# Codex May Goals Progress

Last updated: 2026-05-31 (Cycle 237)

Source of truth: `maygoals.md`

Execution stance: keep moving toward the impossible north star without claiming broad completion from narrow checks. This file is the running ledger for parsed requirements, acceptance criteria, commands, evidence, blockers, and next action.

Status: complete for initial pass.

## Parsed Checklist And Acceptance Criteria

Legend:
- `[ ]` incomplete
- `[~]` partially implemented or foundation exists
- `[x]` verified complete for current scope
- `[blocked]` impossible without credentials, external account access, legal/product decision, or real-world operation

### Global Persistence Rule

- [~] Keep implementing the next highest-leverage primitive instead of stopping at planning.
  - Acceptance: every work cycle selects one incomplete item, changes repo state, runs targeted verification, and records evidence here.
- [~] If a provider is unavailable, build mock/sandbox orchestration and record the blocker now has a service-business `readiness.providerSandboxOrchestrationPlan` and `channels.providerSandbox.plan` that lists AgentPhone, AgentMail, SMS, web-chat, DNS, and vendor-pool provider blockers, maps each unavailable provider to a local dry-run workflow, attaches a `provider-sandbox-orchestration` capability, renders sandbox/live-providers/providers/blockers chips in Portfolio, and explicitly blocks live provider execution, mutation, customer contact, DNS publication, vendor assignment, and spend.
  - Acceptance: provider-gated workflows have deterministic mock/sandbox paths and explicit blocker reporting.
- [~] Claims of readiness must be proven with deterministic checks, durable state, and visible operator/customer evidence now have `readiness.operatingHealthPlan.readinessEvidence` for deterministic check receipts, durable state receipts, operator-visible evidence, and customer-visible evidence gates.
  - Acceptance: readiness commands and UI/API surfaces show dry-run proof, live-smoke proof, blockers, and next actions.

### Non-Negotiable Product Truth

- [~] Preserve customer-facing service framing: customers buy outcomes, agents are internal labor.
  - Acceptance: README, UI copy, routes, and generated customer messages avoid selling "agents" as the product.
- [~] Extend this framing beyond websites into all future service businesses now flows through service-business launch plans for menu, booking, scripts, compliance, customer rooms, acquisition expansion, and operating health instead of website-only delivery.
  - Acceptance: vertical/service-business model carries customer outcome, offer, proof, and fulfillment boundaries.

### Goal 1: City-Scale Market Intelligence

- [~] City and neighborhood demand maps now derive `signals.cityDemandMap` from stored lead `research_json` neighborhood/district/service-area fields or deterministic address-derived corridors. Hotspots carry demand class, hotspot score, lead share, confidence, evidence lead IDs, source URLs, representative niches, and example leads; key fields mirror onto `risks.cityDemandTopNeighborhood`/`risks.cityDemandHotspotCount`/`risks.cityDemandMappedLeadRatio`; Portfolio renders map/top/mapped/hotspot chips; snapshot/export includes the map; and confidence intervals live under `signals.confidenceIntervals.cityDemandMap`. This is local inference only; no live map, census, mobility, permit, weather, or external demand feed is queried.
- [~] Neighborhood launch planning now converts `signals.cityDemandMap` into `signals.neighborhoodLaunchPlan`: top hotspot selection, recommended launch motion, priority score/class, first-wave lead/revenue estimate, proof requirements, evidence lead IDs/source URLs, assumptions, `risks.neighborhoodLaunchKey`/`risks.neighborhoodLaunchMotion`/`risks.neighborhoodLaunchPriorityScore` mirrors, Portfolio start/motion/priority/first-wave chips, snapshot/export serialization, and confidence intervals under `signals.confidenceIntervals.neighborhoodLaunchPlan`. This is local planning only; it does not publish externally, spend money, or make customer promises.
- [~] Vertical opportunity scoring.
- [~] Competitor weakness detection now extracts deterministic weakness signals (no website, weak presence, missing/risky phone, low research confidence, missing source URL, callability uncertain) from lead evidence and persists them on market opportunities for UI/export visibility.
- [~] Review complaint clustering now derives deterministic market-level customer pain clusters from stored `research_json` lead intelligence (`complaintsPainPoints`, review themes, current website issues, missing customer info, review summaries) plus lead notes. Clusters persist under `signals.reviewComplaintClusters`, summarize under `signals.reviewComplaintSummary`, mirror key coverage/risk fields onto `risks`, render as complaint chips in Portfolio, propagate through snapshot/export, and carry confidence intervals under `signals.confidenceIntervals.reviewComplaintClusters`.
- [~] Pricing and margin inference now derives `signals.pricingMarginInference` from stored lead `research_json` pricing/offer/promotion/service-pricing evidence plus lead-intelligence pricing/offer notes before falling back to vertical-pack price. It separates diagnostic fees/coupons/tune-ups from representative service-ticket prices, computes observed price spread, representative price, fulfillment cost, gross margin, confidence, evidence IDs/source URLs, mirrors pricing/margin fields onto `risks`, feeds `unit_economics.representativePriceCents` and market sizing, renders pricing/margin chips in Portfolio, propagates through snapshot/export, and carries uncertainty under `signals.confidenceIntervals.pricingMarginInference`.
- [~] Search-intent capture now derives `signals.searchIntentCapture` from existing lead text/source URLs plus vertical-pack market signals, lead sources, review value props, urgency, demand pressure, and weakness overlap. It classifies intent as `urgent_local_search`/`category_comparison`/`brand_validation`/`booking_or_menu_intent`/`proof_discovery`/`evidence_insufficient`, persists matched intent keys, source coverage, query themes, source alignment, evidence lead ids, required evidence, assumptions, recommended capture surface, and `source: lead_evidence_search_intent_capture`; mirrors `risks.searchIntentClass`/`risks.recommendedCaptureSurface`/`risks.searchIntentScore`/`risks.searchIntentEvidenceRequired`; surfaces compact intent/surface/fit chips; and includes the captured intent score in `signals.confidenceIntervals`.
- [~] Formation/permit signal ingestion where available now mines stored lead `research_json`/source evidence for license, permit/inspection, insurance/bond, business-formation, recent permit activity, and missing license-proof signals. Results persist under `signals.formationPermitSignals` and `signals.formationPermitSummary`, mirror coverage/risk/evidence requirements onto `risks`, render as formation/permit chips in Portfolio, propagate through snapshot/export, and carry confidence intervals under `signals.confidenceIntervals.formationPermitSignals`. This is local ingestion only; no live government registry lookup runs.
- [~] Local seasonality modeling now infers deterministic seasonal windows such as `summer_cooling_ramp` from aggregation month, vertical-pack market/growth/retention text, and stored lead evidence. Results persist under `signals.localSeasonality`, mirror class/window/multiplier/evidence requirements onto `risks`, render as season/window/lift chips in Portfolio, propagate through snapshot/export, and carry a seasonal-pressure confidence interval under `signals.confidenceIntervals.localSeasonality`. This is local inference only; no live weather, ad-auction, SERP, or external demand feed is queried.
- [~] Ad saturation and offer fatigue detection now mines stored lead `research_json`, paid-search/search-result/offer evidence, lead-intelligence ad observations, promotions, complaints, website issues, and lead notes for paid ad density, aggregator crowding, discount fatigue, generic claim fatigue, and trust-claim fatigue. Results persist under `signals.adSaturationOfferFatigue`, mirror saturation/fatigue/composite scores and evidence requirements onto `risks`, render as ads/fatigue/pressure/positioning chips in Portfolio, propagate through snapshot/export, and carry confidence intervals under `signals.confidenceIntervals.adSaturationOfferFatigue`. This is local inference only; no live ad library, SERP, auction, social-ad, or keyword-volume provider is queried.
- [~] Owner responsiveness prediction now derives a deterministic `responsivenessClass` (`responsive_likely`/`mixed`/`unresponsive_risk`/`evidence_insufficient`) and `recommendedAcquisitionMotion` (`call_ready`/`inbound_first`/`proof_first`/`evidence_review`) from existing lead `risk_status`, `phone_classification`, `phone`, `callable_reason`, `website`, `source_url`, `presence_confidence`, `online_presence_strength`, urgency class, and demand pressure. Persisted on `signals.ownerResponsiveness` with `responseFrictionScore`, `callableCoverageRatio`, `businessPhoneCoverageRatio`, `phonePresentRatio`, matched negative/positive signal keys (`no_callback`/`missed_call`/`unanswered`/`no_response`/`slow_response` vs `fast_response`/`callback_path`/`response_time_promise`/`business_landline_evidence`), per-key evidence lead ids, blockers/evidence required, assumptions, inputs, weights, exploit narrative, and `source: lead_evidence_owner_responsiveness_prediction`; mirrored onto `risks.ownerResponsivenessClass`/`risks.recommendedAcquisitionMotion`/`risks.responseFrictionScore`/`risks.ownerResponsivenessBlockers`; surfaced as compact owners/motion/friction/callable/needs chips in the Portfolio opportunity row; propagated through snapshot and ops export. `predictOwnerResponsiveness` is exported for direct deterministic unit testing without DB state.
- [~] Service urgency classification now derives a deterministic `emergency_first`/`urgent_response`/`planned_service`/`discretionary` class from lead niche/business name/callable reason/source URL/online presence text plus vertical pack valuePropHook/customerPersonaHint/marketSignals/growthPaths/restrictedClaims using a weighted lexicon, persists it on `market_opportunities.signals.serviceUrgency` with response-expectation minutes, response requirements, evidence lead ids, pack alignment, and an exploit recommendation, and mirrors urgency class plus response requirements into `risks.serviceUrgencyClass`/`risks.serviceUrgencyResponseRequirements`. Market demand pressure now scores `low`/`moderate`/`elevated`/`high` from urgency, exploitable competitor weakness share, no-website overlap, callable responder gap, lead density, weak-presence majority, and urgency-aligned vertical growth paths, persisted on `signals.demandPressure` with drivers, evidence, sources, and an exploitation strategy and mirrored into `risks.demandPressureLevel`/`risks.demandPressureDriverKeys`.
- [~] TAM, SAM, immediately-callable estimates now derive a deterministic `signals.marketSizing` from existing lead evidence and vertical-pack margin model: `totalObservedLeads`, `immediatelyCallableLeads`, `weakPresenceCallableLeads`, `representativeTicketCents`, `estimatedFulfillmentCostCents`, `contributionMarginCents`, `maxAcquisitionCostCents`, `estimatedCallableRevenueCents`, `serviceableAvailableMarketCents` (SAM proxy), `obtainableFirstWaveLeads`/`obtainableFirstWaveCents`/`obtainableFirstWaveMarginCents` (SOM proxy from urgency, demand pressure, exploitable weakness, evidence confidence), `firstWaveConversionRate`, `confidence`, `assumptions`, `evidenceLeadIds`, and `source: lead_evidence_market_sizing_estimate`. Mirrored on `unit_economics.marketSizing` and on `risks.marketSizingConfidence`/`risks.obtainableFirstWaveCents`/`risks.serviceableAvailableMarketCents`; surfaced as compact callable/SAM/SOM/confidence chips in the Portfolio opportunity row; propagated through snapshot and ops export.
- [~] Confidence intervals for every market-intelligence claim now persist under `signals.confidenceIntervals`: score, aggregate confidence, exploitable weakness ratio, demand pressure score, owner-response friction, search-intent score, review-complaint coverage/top-cluster score, formation/permit coverage/regulatory-risk score, local-seasonality pressure score, ad-saturation/offer-fatigue composite/saturation/fatigue scores, city-demand mapped-lead ratio/top-hotspot score, pricing confidence/price spread/representative price/gross margin, neighborhood-launch priority/first-wave revenue, market-recommendation explainability, callable revenue, SAM, SOM, and first-wave margin carry low/point/high bands, half-width, width class, sample size, evidence count, input-agreement score, method, assumptions, and `source: lead_evidence_confidence_interval_propagation`; the summary mirrors onto `risks.confidenceIntervalWidthClass`/`risks.confidenceIntervalSpeculative`/`risks.claimConfidenceFloor` and surfaces as compact uncertainty/score-CI/SAM-CI chips in the Portfolio row.
- [~] Source evidence for every lead, vertical, and market recommendation now has a durable market-level provenance ledger. `signals.marketRecommendationProvenance` links stored lead rows/source URLs, vertical-pack rules, derived market signals, pricing, city-demand map, and neighborhood launch plan into source models, decision inputs, top reasons, evidence chain, evidence coverage, explainability score/class, assumptions, and `source: lead_evidence_market_recommendation_provenance`; key values mirror onto `risks.marketRecommendationExplainabilityScore`/`risks.marketRecommendationTopReasonKeys`/`risks.marketRecommendationEvidenceRequired`; Portfolio renders why/explained/proof/reason chips; snapshot/export include it; and false-positive learning updates the provenance decision/score so stale launch reasoning is not preserved after a downgrade.
- [~] Automatically launch the first acquisition motion from a market recommendation. `planLaunchFromMarketOpportunity` now refreshes acquisition strategy for a new launch candidate, creates the `launch_first_motion` owned-surface action, auto-approves only the zero-spend local dry-run scope with market-opportunity evidence, executes the dry-run through `owned_surface_builder`, records a provider receipt, marks the service-business channel `launch_ready`, and returns idempotently without duplicate receipts on repeat planning. Live/public/external promotion remains gated.
- [~] Learn from false positives and market failures now records operator/proof outcomes through `recordMarketRecommendationOutcome()` and `POST /api/portfolio/market-opportunities/:id/record-outcome`. The loop appends `signals.marketOutcomeLearning`, creates a `portfolio_learning_records` row with `source_type: market_opportunity_outcome`, mirrors false-positive counts, reason keys, score penalty, and decision override onto `risks`, moves the opportunity to `learning_review`, renders learned/penalty/next/reason chips in Portfolio, and reapplies the learned penalty on future aggregation before launch decisions.

Acceptance: Callan can persist market opportunities by city/territory/vertical, cite evidence, score launchability, decide avoid/launch/watch, and feed the selected opportunity into business creation/acquisition workflows. Current status: `market_opportunities` now exists and `npm run check:maygoals` proves direct opportunity evidence, lead-evidence aggregation from existing city/niche leads into market opportunities with vertical-pack unit economics, deterministic competitor-weakness/review-complaint/formation-permit/urgency/demand-pressure/market-sizing/owner-responsiveness/search-intent/confidence-interval/ad-fatigue/seasonality signals, automatic first-acquisition-motion dry-run execution from a selected recommendation, and false-positive outcome learning that penalizes repeat launch recommendations; broader market ingestion and live external promotion remain incomplete.

### Goal 2: Business Creation From Zero

- [~] Autonomous brand generation.
- [~] Local domain strategy now has a provenance-derived `readiness.localDomainStrategyPlan` and `channels.localDomain.plan` on service-business launch candidates. The plan turns brand/city/vertical/neighborhood/trust/booking/menu/comms evidence into owned-domain candidates, a Callan-local draft URL, route path, DNS/TLS/email-authentication plan, proof blockers, evidence lead IDs/source URLs, and `source: market_provenance_local_domain_strategy_plan`; attaches a `local-domain-strategy-plan` capability; renders domain/registration/DNS/primary/path chips in Portfolio; snapshot/export includes it; and blocks domain registration, DNS mutation, certificate issuance, external publication, local ownership claims, and customer-facing domain promises until availability, ownership, brand-conflict, NAP, SSL, provider-link, and operator approvals exist.
- [~] Launch readiness runbook generation now rolls trust assets, booking flow, service menu, phone/inbox, local domain, and vertical-manifest checklist evidence into `readiness.launchReadinessWorkItemPlan` and `channels.launchReadiness.plan`. The plan creates six local operator work items for trust proof, booking intake, offer approval, communications provider proof, domain proof, and the final operator launch gate; records required proof/setup keys, prerequisite plan sources, evidence lead IDs/source URLs, and `source: market_provenance_launch_readiness_work_item_plan`; attaches a `launch-readiness-work-items` capability; renders work-items/live-work/blocked/draft/item chips in Portfolio; snapshot/export includes it; and blocks external jobs, provider mutations, publication, domain reservation, calls, messages, dispatch, and spend until launch gates pass.
- [~] Phone number and inbox provisioning now has a provenance-derived `readiness.communicationProvisioningPlan`, `channels.communicationProvisioning.plan`, `channels.inboundCall.provisioningPlan`, and `channels.ownerInbox.provisioningPlan` on service-business launch candidates. The plan defines AgentPhone local-number/provider-link requirements, AgentMail inbox/provider-link requirements, recording disclosure, DNC/quiet-hours, opt-out/privacy, routing rules for booking intake, missed-call follow-up, and quote/policy recap; records required setup keys, evidence lead IDs/source URLs, assumptions, and `source: market_provenance_communication_provisioning_plan`; attaches a `communication-provisioning-plan` capability; renders comms/live-comms/phone/inbox/routing chips in Portfolio; snapshot/export includes it; and blocks number reservation, inbox creation, calls, emails, recording, and response-time promises until provider proof and operator approval exist.
- [~] Service menu generation now has a provenance-derived `readiness.serviceMenuPlan`, `offer.serviceMenuPlan`, and `channels.serviceMenu.plan` on service-business launch candidates. The plan turns vertical-pack packages, local pricing/margin inference, selected neighborhood, booking-flow steps, and trust assets into draft menu sections and package drafts; records public-menu/public-price blockers, area, representative price, margin context, evidence lead IDs/source URLs, required setup keys, and `source: market_provenance_service_menu_plan`; attaches a `service-menu-draft` capability; renders menu/public-menu/price/package chips in Portfolio; snapshot/export includes it; and creates no payment link, public menu page, quote, discount, or price promise.
- [~] Pricing and package design now flows from stored price evidence into `signals.pricingMarginInference`, service-business `offer.serviceMenuPlan`, package drafts, pricing disclosure trust assets, and booking-flow price-boundary acknowledgement. Live customer-facing price changes remain blocked until operator review and launch gates pass.
- [~] Booking flow setup now starts as a provenance-derived `readiness.bookingFlowPlan` and `channels.bookingFlow.plan` on service-business launch candidates. The plan defines service-area check, problem intake, urgency triage, contact permission, price-boundary acknowledgement, and operator-review steps; records intake fields, required setup keys, evidence lead IDs/source URLs, area, price, urgency, customer-promise boundaries, and `source: market_provenance_booking_flow_plan`; attaches a `booking-flow-draft` capability; renders booking/live-booking/area/steps/fields/step-status chips in Portfolio; snapshot/export includes it; and blocks external booking, calendar reservations, dispatch, confirmations, and emergency/customer promises until provider smoke, phone/inbox setup, calendar adapter proof, and operator approval exist.
- [~] Payment collection exists for website service through Stripe.
- [~] Customer portal exists for website/build workflow.
- [~] Initial landing page and SEO pages for new service businesses.
- [~] Scripts for phone, email, SMS, chat, and human subcontractors now have a provenance-derived `readiness.serviceScriptPlan` and `channels.serviceScripts.plan` on service-business launch candidates. The plan turns booking-flow, service-menu, communication-provisioning, local-domain, and launch-work-item evidence into five blocked draft scripts (`phone_booking_intake_script`, `email_quote_recap_template`, `sms_status_update_template`, `web_chat_intake_script`, `subcontractor_dispatch_handoff_script`); records proof/setup keys, prerequisite plan sources, evidence lead IDs/source URLs, and `source: market_provenance_service_script_plan`; attaches a `service-script-drafts` capability; renders scripts/live-scripts/messages/vendors/channel-status chips in Portfolio; snapshot/export includes it; and blocks calls, email/SMS sends, chat publication, customer-data exposure, vendor assignment, dispatch promises, and response-time promises until provider proof and operator approval exist.
- [~] Reputation/review capture has pieces through growth/aftercare.
- [~] Service-area configuration.
- [~] Trust page, refund policy, privacy copy, and proof assets now have a provenance-derived `readiness.trustAssetPlan` on service-business launch candidates. The plan lists draft/evidence-ready/operator-review assets for trust page, pricing disclosure, service-area proof, privacy notice, refund/remediation policy, and license/restricted-claim review; mirrors into the `trust-and-compliance-review` capability; adds required trust/proof keys to launch readiness; carries evidence lead IDs/source URLs/provenance context; renders trust-assets/evidence/area/asset-status chips in Portfolio; snapshot/export includes it; and keeps public claims/external publication/live promises blocked until gates and operator approval pass.
- [~] Launch-readiness checks before public promotion.
- [~] Operator approval gates for legal, compliance, and brand-sensitive steps.

Acceptance: a detected opportunity can become a persisted service business with brand, territory, offer, channels, portal, payment path, readiness gates, and reversible/auditable launch state. Current status: a detected opportunity can now become a persisted `service_businesses` launch candidate with brand, territory, offer, channel/readiness JSON, capabilities, workflows, vendors, portfolio events, gate receipts, launch surfaces, post-launch monitoring, and a Callan-owned public surface route. Domain/phone/inbox/provisioning and live external publication remain incomplete.

### Goal 4: Real Fulfillment, Not Just Digital Delivery

- [~] Software fulfillment for websites, contact forms, commerce setup, analytics/schema/contact readiness, intake, follow-up.
- [~] Fulfillment capability registry.
- [~] Vendor/subcontractor onboarding.
- [~] Vendor qualification.
- [~] Insurance/license requirement tracking now has dispatch preflight receipts and vendor compliance blockers.
- [~] Service territory matching.
- [~] Calendar and dispatch now have dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts and live booking/field-dispatch blockers.
- [~] Customer operating-room job tracking now has dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts, vendor-ETA/photo-permission/status-copy/customer-visibility/portal-access blockers, and live tracker-publication blockers.
- [~] Job acceptance/status/customer updates now have dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts, contact/consent/provider-link checks, and live-message blockers.
- [~] Photo/proof collection and completion verification now has completion-proof dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts and proof/customer-acceptance blockers.
- [~] Refund/dispute workflows now have dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts and live refund/chargeback blockers.
- [~] Vendor quality scoring and backup vendor routing now have dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts, deterministic scoring, backup-vendor proof, and live reassignment blockers.
- [~] Margin-aware assignment now has dispatch preflight payout/cost-cap checks.
- [~] SLA tracking now has completion-proof preflight blockers for arrival/completion/update evidence.
- [~] Repeat-work scheduling now has dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts, completion/customer-update dependencies, opt-in/offer/window blockers, and live reminder/booking/invoice/subscription blockers.

Acceptance: Callan can sell and coordinate non-digital service delivery with proof, SLAs, vendor state, customer updates, and margin. Current status: vendor candidates and capability registry rows can be persisted for a launch candidate, safe-to-fulfill gates can allow or block jobs, booking/dispatch receipts prove dry-run planning, live preflight blockers, vendor license/insurance checks, payment/scope checks, payout/cost-cap margin boundaries, calendar/customer/vendor evidence blockers, live-smoke blockers, and rollback without creating bookings, reserving calendar slots, assigning vendors, promising customers, or starting field dispatch; customer operating-room job-tracking receipts now prove dry-run planning, live preflight blockers, dispatch/customer/vendor checks, vendor ETA/photo permission/status copy/customer visibility/portal access blockers, customer-portal live-smoke blockers, and rollback without tracker publication, ETA promises, photo exposure, customer messages, vendor GPS sharing, or job mutation; customer status-update receipts now prove dry-run planning, live preflight blockers, contact/consent/provider-link checks, message approval/ETA/SLA evidence blockers, AgentMail live-smoke blockers, and rollback without email, SMS, call, chat, or job-status mutation; completion-proof receipts now prove dry-run planning, live preflight blockers, dispatch receipt dependency, proof/SLA/customer-acceptance/payout blockers, AgentMail live-smoke blockers, and rollback without completing jobs, sending customer notices, requesting reviews, capturing payment, or releasing vendor payout; payout-settlement receipts now prove dry-run planning, live preflight blockers, completion receipt dependency, safe-to-charge/payment/Stripe/vendor/margin gates, vendor-invoice/customer-acceptance/refund-dispute evidence blockers, Stripe live-smoke blockers, and rollback without capture, transfer, payout, refund, or chargeback side effects; refund/dispute receipts now prove dry-run review, live preflight blockers, payout receipt dependency, amount boundary, customer issue/policy/handoff/vendor-resolution/operator approval blockers, Stripe live-smoke blockers, and rollback without refund, chargeback acceptance, customer notice, vendor penalty, or payment mutation; vendor-quality receipts now prove primary/backup vendor scoring, backup vendor selection evidence, live preflight blockers for customer update/vendor acceptance/SLA reassignment proof, AgentPhone live-smoke blockers, and rollback without reassignment, customer notices, vendor penalties, or calendar changes; repeat-work receipts now prove dry-run scheduling after completion/customer update context, live preflight blockers for completed-service/offer/opt-in/window proof, AgentMail live-smoke blockers, and rollback without creating reminders, bookings, invoices, subscriptions, customer promises, or job mutations. Real provider-backed calendar booking, vendor acceptance, field completion, proof capture, live payout/refund execution, and live SLA workflows remain incomplete.

### Goal 5: Universal Workflow Engine

- [~] Durable jobs, leases, retries, idempotency exist.
- [~] Durable workflow definitions beyond individual job types.
- [~] Typed events now include workflow entity links for several core domains, but not universal workflow events.
- [~] Idempotent commands exist in many paths.
- [~] Long-running state machines now have versioned workflow instances, ordered events, and migration history, but not universal adapter coverage.
- [~] Compensation actions now cover acquisition, fulfillment receipts, provider fallback, and workflow replay receipt rollback, but not every workflow instance.
- [~] Human approval steps exist in account manager/customer portal/live calls.
- [~] Provider fallback steps now have dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts, primary-provider link checks, fallback-route proof, incident/approval/handoff blockers, and live adapter blockers.
- [~] Dead-letter queues now have durable workflow replay receipts for failed/exhausted job evidence and are visible in the Portfolio operator queue panel as a workflow replay subqueue with dry-run/live-preflight/rollback status, source-job dead-letter proof, operator replay approval blockers, and live-adapter blockers.
- [~] Replay tools now have dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts and live replay blockers for durable jobs, but no live replay adapter.
- [~] Timeline reconstruction now exists for workflow instances plus linked service/customer/job/payment/finance entities, lead/trust/handoff, but not every domain is attached yet.
- [~] Per-step cost/latency/evidence now exists in workflow instance events and many provider receipts.
- [~] Versioned workflow schemas now have instance migration support.
- [~] Deterministic mock mode is enforced for workflow instances, but not every live workflow has parity coverage.
- [~] Current workflows cover lead discovery, research, scoring, outreach, calls, inbound intake, quote, invoice/payment, fulfillment, QA, revision, launch, hosting, growth, aftercare, escalations, subscription renewal.
- [~] Workflows cover vendor dispatch, customer operating-room job tracking, completion proof, payout settlement, refund/dispute review, vendor-quality routing, repeat-work scheduling, and provider fallback routing partially; churn recovery is not a first-class path yet.

Acceptance: any customer, job, payment, build, vendor assignment, or conversation can be explained from first event to current state without guessing. Current status: `workflow_definitions` and `portfolio_events` now provide a portfolio-level definition/event layer, `workflow_instances` and `workflow_instance_events` now record versioned state-machine instances, ordered state transitions, commands, decisions, evidence, cost, latency, deterministic mock mode, explainable timelines, and definition-version migrations; `workflow_entity_links` now attach service businesses, customers, jobs, payments, and finance rollups back to workflow timelines through `explainEntity`; acquisition actions now have a verified local compensation/rollback path, job-tracking receipts now prove task-scoped customer operating-room preflight/rollback state, provider fallback receipts now prove a primary provider, fallback route, workflow scope, live blockers, and rollback for a task-scoped fallback plan, and `workflow_replay_receipts` now prove dead-letter replay dry-run/preflight/rollback for terminal durable jobs without enqueueing replacements, invoking handlers, or mutating source jobs and surface in the Portfolio operator desk; universal workflow instances/replay/compensation across every domain are not done.

### Goal 6: Multi-Tenant Agency And Holding-Company Platform

- [~] Organizations.
- [~] Workspaces.
- [~] Roles now have tenant role assignments and access-governance receipts, but no universal auth middleware or live identity-provider adapter.
- [~] Operators.
- [~] Customers now have scoped `customer_contact` access policy receipts and live preflight blockers, but no full cross-service customer identity model.
- [~] Vendors.
- [~] Service businesses.
- [~] Territories.
- [~] Brands.
- [~] Vertical packs exist, but not business-in-a-box.
- [~] Provider credentials per tenant now have dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts, workspace/service/account-key ownership proof, no-secret-material safety proof, live-smoke blockers, and provider readiness gates require credential ownership.
- [~] Data/customer isolation now has tenant isolation audit receipts, second-workspace probe evidence, snapshot scope checks, relationship mismatch checks, live-preflight blockers, and rollback without data motion.
- [~] Audit logs exist but are lead-centric.
- [~] Tenant-level billing now has local billing-plan receipts and live billing blockers, but no real tenant charging adapter.
- [~] Usage limits now have workspace control receipts and live enforcement blockers, but no live ejection/alert adapter.
- [~] Feature flags now have workspace control receipts and live-change blockers, but no full flag service.
- [~] Run modes per tenant now have workspace policy receipts and live-promotion blockers.
- [~] Compliance policies per tenant/geography now have workspace control receipts, required-region policy proof, and live-publication blockers.
- [~] Tenant backups/exports/deletion/retention now have scoped export/backup/retention/deletion-hold receipts, live-preflight blockers, and rollback, but no live backup/export/delete adapter.

Acceptance: many distinct businesses can run without data leakage, brand confusion, provider cross-talk, or operator overload. Current status: first SQLite tables/accessors exist, provider links now require tenant-scoped credential ownership proof before they count as ready, provider credential receipts prove no secret read/persistence/mutation/cross-tenant use, tenant isolation receipts audit scoped customer/contact/job/payment/provider/vendor/surface relationships plus snapshot exclusion of a second workspace probe, tenant operating-control receipts now plan billing, usage limits, feature flags, run-mode policy, and geography/compliance policy with live enforcement blockers, tenant lifecycle receipts now plan scoped redacted exports, backup intent, retention policy, and deletion holds with live export/delete/backups blocked, and tenant role/access receipts now prove operator-owner and customer-contact permissions, least-privilege policy, customer portal privacy, live-access blockers, and rollback without auth-provider/customer-data/provider-credential side effects; live billing/usage/flag/lifecycle/access enforcement adapters are not done.

### Goal 7: Autonomous Finance And Capital Allocation

- [~] Lead-level cost/margin exists.
- [~] Margin by service.
- [~] Margin by market now has durable `market_finance` rollups, but not live accounting reconciliation.
- [~] Provider cost attribution partially exists.
- [~] Human labor cost attribution now has explicit cost entries tied to workspace/service/job/operator/market, but no payroll/provider sync.
- [~] Vendor payout tracking now has payout-settlement dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts.
- [~] Refunds and chargebacks now have refund/dispute dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts and live-money blockers.
- [~] CAC by channel.
- [~] LTV by segment now has durable `segment_ltv` rollups, but not cohort-level retention curves.
- [~] Payback period now derives from CAC and contribution margin in finance rollups, but not calendar-cohort cash timing.
- [~] Subscription retention partial.
- [~] Expansion revenue now uses repeat-work signals in finance rollups, but not live subscription/account expansion.
- [~] Churn prediction now has deterministic churn-risk scoring from refund/incident/repeat/update signals, but no trained model.
- [~] Price testing now has offer-bundle experiment guardrails, but no live pricing experiment runner.
- [~] Offer bundling now has durable finance-backed bundle plans, but no live payment-link or customer-offer rollout.
- [~] Margin-aware routing now appears as a finance recommendation, but does not yet auto-route providers/jobs.
- [~] Capital allocation recommendations, locally executable action receipts, and market-level allocation dry-run/preflight/rollback receipts.
- [~] Shut-down recommendations now appear as finance review candidates when contribution margin and acquisition budget fail, but no service is shut down automatically.
- [~] Market expansion recommendations now appear as `scale_market` rollups, but no market launch happens automatically.

Acceptance: Callan recommends where to invest, pause, shut down, price differently, or change providers using durable economics. Current status: portfolio acquisition attempts now roll up first-customer spend, conversion rate, revenue, margin, cost per attempt, cost per conversion, and launch-budget fit by service business, then upsert a durable scale/watch/optimize/pause recommendation, service-business channel strategy, conversion-monitoring summary, learning record, operator-reviewed acquisition action queue, and proof-preserving provider dry-run receipt; market-level capital-allocation receipts now allocate a declared budget to proven scale candidates, hold unproven services, block live budget moves on operator approval/runway/live-smoke/adapter gates, and rollback workspace allocation metadata without ad spend or provider budget mutation; payout settlement records vendor payout amount, expected margin, payment status, and live-money blockers in durable receipts; refund/dispute receipts record requested refund exposure, payment boundary, policy/approval blockers, and live chargeback/refund blockers; labor-cost entries and finance rollups now compute market margin, service contribution margin, segment LTV, payback, expansion revenue, churn risk, margin-aware routing, market expansion, and shutdown-review recommendations without billing, budget, price, or shutdown side effects; offer-bundle plans now attach components, pricing, expected margin, finance rollup source, and pricing-test guardrails without payment links or live price changes. Live external execution and real accounting reconciliation remain incomplete.

### Goal 8: Trust, Safety, Compliance, Reputation

- [~] Consent ledger, opt-out, DNC, quiet hours, recording disclosures, webhook authenticity, replay protection, provider incidents, emergency stop, reputation monitoring, human review have foundations.
- [~] Region-specific policies now have a launch-candidate `readiness.serviceCompliancePolicyPlan` and `channels.compliancePolicy.plan` that infer jurisdiction candidates from stored city/source evidence, record region policy drafts for jurisdiction verification, license/permit claims, and communications disclosures, require operator verification, render policy/live-claims/region/restricted chips in Portfolio, and block public claims until proof and approval exist.
- [~] Vertical-specific restricted advice across services now flows from vertical-pack `compliance.restrictedClaims`/`requiredDisclosures` into `readiness.serviceCompliancePolicyPlan`, restricted advice rules, required proof keys, a `service-compliance-policy-plan` capability, snapshot/export coverage, and explicit blockers for license, 24/7 emergency, rebate/financing, recording-disclosure, and operator-claim approvals. The plan is internal guidance only and does not publish legal, license, emergency, warranty, price, or scope claims.
- [~] Claims verification exists in build QA/trust but not universal.
- [~] No fake reviews/credentials/business history policy exists implicitly; needs explicit cross-service enforcement.
- [~] Customer data minimization and PII redaction exist in ops/logging.
- [~] Audit exports exist.
- [~] Rollback as a general primitive.
- [~] Complaint triage and post-incident learning now has refund/dispute receipt blockers plus incident-learning records, but no universal complaint queue.

Acceptance: autonomy can increase without increasing hidden risk; every risky action has policy, ledger, blocker, or human approval path. Current status: acquisition action execution now has a local rollback/compensation path with provider receipts, but rollback is not yet universal across calls, email, payments, public pages, vendor dispatch, and customer jobs.

- [~] Quote, scope approval, payment, intake, asset URL, build preview, revision, launch approval, callback, invoices, support thread, growth, aftercare, privacy/opt-out, trust timeline exist for website flow.
- [~] Booking management for all services now has a launch-candidate `readiness.customerOperatingRoomPlan`/`channels.customerOperatingRoom.plan` workflow that records booking-management proof keys and blocks customer-visible booking until booking copy, calendar live smoke, and operator approval exist.

- [~] Vendor ETA now appears in `readiness.customerOperatingRoomPlan.workflows` as a blocked customer-room workflow requiring vendor acceptance, ETA evidence, SLA boundaries, and operator approval before any customer-facing ETA promise.
- [~] Photos and proof now appear in `readiness.customerOperatingRoomPlan.workflows` as a blocked completion-proof workflow requiring photo permission, completion proof, privacy review, and customer-visibility approval before proof/photo exposure.
- [~] Privacy/opt-out controls now persist `portfolio_customer_privacy_control_receipts`, reject live mode, record opt-out copy, privacy notice, token-scoped portal access, data-minimization, photo-redaction, vendor-GPS, and operator approval blockers, and prove no portal publication, customer message, customer-data exposure, photo exposure, vendor-GPS sharing, or job-status mutation occurred.
- [~] Subscription management partial through hosting.
- [~] Review requests across services now appear in `readiness.customerOperatingRoomPlan.workflows` as a blocked review-request workflow requiring completed-service proof, no-incentive/no-gating policy, customer opt-in, and operator approval before any review request is sent.

Acceptance: a stranger can buy, track, revise, approve, renew, and get support for any service without founder explanation. Current status: launch-candidate customer operating-room plans now define blocked cross-service workflows, and durable privacy/opt-out receipts prove the portal cannot expose customer data, photos, vendor GPS, customer messages, or job-status mutations before explicit local blockers are cleared.

### Goal 14: Acquisition And Roll-Up Engine

- [~] Business performance diagnosis now appears in `readiness.acquisitionExpansionPlan.diagnostics` as a draft, evidence-gated M&A diagnostic derived from market provenance and launch plans.
- [~] Owner outreach now appears in `readiness.acquisitionExpansionPlan.workflows` with consent, owner identity verification, and operator approval blockers; live owner contact remains blocked.
- [~] Revenue uplift estimate now appears as a draft diagnostic with representative-price/first-wave/margin inputs and actual revenue/conversion/pricing review blockers.
- [~] Operational gap report now appears as a draft diagnostic for missed callbacks, booking clarity, proof assets, and provider reliability, with owner-process/customer-issue/SLA proof blockers.
- [~] Acquisition target scoring now derives a deterministic draft target score and score band from provenance, demand pressure, margin, and owner-responsiveness signals.
- [~] Partnership offer generation now has dry-run safety receipts and live outreach blockers.
- [~] LOI workflow support now appears as a blocked M&A workflow requiring legal review, valuation assumption review, and operator signature gates.
- [~] Due diligence checklist now appears as a draft M&A workflow requiring financial statement, vendor contract, and liability review proof.
- [~] Data-room intake now appears as a blocked workflow requiring privacy review, access policy, and redaction policy proof before any confidential access.
- [~] Integration plan now appears as a draft workflow requiring systems inventory plus customer/vendor migration reviews.
- [~] Transition playbook now appears as a draft workflow requiring owner-transition SOP, customer-communication review, and handoff timeline proof.
- [~] Brand preservation plan now appears as a draft workflow requiring brand-history, review-profile ownership, and public-claim review proof.
- [~] Post-acquisition automation rollout now appears as a blocked workflow requiring automation-scope, customer-disruption, and rollback review.

Acceptance: Callan can identify, evaluate, absorb, and improve SMBs, with evidence for "we can improve, operate, and measure the gain." Current status: service-business launch candidates now carry a local acquisition expansion plan with business diagnosis, uplift estimates, gap reporting, target scoring, owner outreach, LOI, due diligence, data-room, integration, transition, brand-preservation, and post-acquisition rollout workflows; the plan is visible in Portfolio and covered by deterministic checks, but it does not contact owners, send LOIs, access data rooms, acquire businesses, migrate customers/vendors, or automate acquired businesses.

### Goal 15: Self-Inspecting Company

- [~] Safe-to-sell, provider freshness, webhook freshness, smoke freshness, queue health, margin health, reputation health, compliance health, backup health, eval health, incident history, next actions, and portfolio incident learning exist.
- [~] Safe-to-call/email/charge/fulfill/launch/renew/expand as explicit portfolio-level gates.
- [~] Customer SLA health now appears in `readiness.operatingHealthPlan.healthChecks` with response, booking, status-update, and completion-proof measures plus receipt blockers.
- [~] Vendor SLA health now appears in `readiness.operatingHealthPlan.healthChecks` with acceptance, arrival-window, completion-quality, and backup-route measures plus vendor-history blockers.
- [~] Stop-before-danger and accelerate-when-green exists partially through readiness/safe-to-sell, portfolio acquisition strategy recommendations, and reviewed acquisition action execution.

Acceptance: Callan can inspect whether the company is allowed to make money today and explain why/why not. Current status: service businesses now persist `safe_to_launch`, `safe_to_fulfill`, and `safe_to_charge` gate receipts with blockers and evidence, launch requires a truthful live acquisition surface plus monitoring checks, and scale/hold/channel-test actions require operator review, dry-run proof, live preflight, run-mode/side-effect gates, and provider-specific smoke/target/ownership/widget/source/partner proof before any live-capable publication, call motion, email motion, SMS motion, chat widget publication, referral link publication, partnership outreach, or external Google profile mutation; `readiness.operatingHealthPlan` now adds customer SLA, vendor SLA, vertical health, provider fabric, deterministic proof, durable-state proof, operator-visible evidence, and customer-visible evidence gates, but renew/expand gates and automated external promotion remain incomplete.

### Goal 16: Autonomous Product Evolution

- [~] Product telemetry now has a launch-candidate `readiness.productTelemetryPlan`/`channels.productTelemetry.plan` with draft telemetry streams, prerequisite plan sources, required schema/review gates, snapshot/export coverage, and Portfolio telemetry chips.
- [~] Feature bottleneck detection now exists as a `productTelemetryPlan.telemetryStreams` entry that captures blocked work item count, provider blocker count, and customer visibility blocker count before generating reviewed product specs.
- [~] Operator frustration capture now exists as a `productTelemetryPlan.telemetryStreams` entry for reopened escalations, bulk-review rejection, and manual override reasons; live capture remains blocked until privacy/operator gates exist.
- [~] Customer confusion capture now exists as a `productTelemetryPlan.telemetryStreams` entry for unclear price boundary, booking state, and ETA state signals; live customer telemetry remains blocked.
- [~] Broken workflow detection now exists as a `productTelemetryPlan.telemetryStreams` entry for missing prerequisites, stale blockers, and failed dry-run receipts, feeding draft bug reports/regression checks only.
- [~] Missing integration detection now exists as a `productTelemetryPlan.telemetryStreams` entry for provider-link, live-smoke, and adapter-implementation gaps, feeding draft eval/migration artifacts only.
- [~] Auto-generated bug reports/eval cases/product specs/migration plans now exist as `productTelemetryPlan.generatedArtifacts` in draft-only state with required review gates before publication or work creation.
- [~] Safe PR proposal generation now has a `safe_pr_proposal_gate_required` blocker and `automaticPrProposalAllowed=false` in `productTelemetryPlan`; no PR is opened automatically.
- [~] Regression-proofing before merge now has a draft `regression_proofing_check` generated artifact and `regression_proofing_gate_required` blocker.
- [~] Notice repeated customer/operator/build/provider/market/portal patterns and convert them into work now has `productTelemetryPlan.patternDetectors` for repeated provider blockers, customer visibility blockers, operator escalations, and integration gaps; conversion remains review-gated.

Acceptance: operating evidence drives what software should be built next.

### Grand Unified Data Model

- [~] First-class model for Organization, Workspace, Brand, Territory, Service business, Vendor, Operator, Capability, Workflow, Event, Evidence, and Offer/service-readiness JSON.
- [~] First-class model for Vertical, Customer, Lead linkage, Contact, Conversation, Consent, Quote, Invoice, Payment, Subscription, Job, Fulfillment task, Provider, Decision, Memory, Handoff, Incident, Experiment, Eval, Policy, Margin, Reputation, Asset, Portal session, Approval.
- [~] First-class gate-evaluation receipts now connect service businesses to approval, provider, payment, vendor, incident, job, and task evidence.
- [~] First-class launch-surface and monitoring-check receipts now connect launched service businesses to live URLs, proof, metrics, and post-launch checks.
- [~] First-class acquisition-attempt receipts now connect launched service businesses to channel, launch surface, customer/lead, cost, revenue, margin, attribution, evidence, conversion tracking, and budget-fit rollups.
- [~] First-class strategy-recommendation receipts now connect acquisition economics to scale/watch/optimize/pause decisions, channel-plan updates, monitoring evidence, and learning records.
- [~] First-class acquisition-action receipts now connect strategy recommendations to review status, guardrails, budget, proof, rollback state, channel execution state, and operator evidence.
- [~] First-class provider dry-run, live-preflight, first-party live-publish, external-provider blocker, and rollback receipts now connect acquisition actions to provider, mode, request, response, launch surface or call/email/profile/SMS/chat/referral/partnership safety proof, cost, rollback, and evidence payloads.

Acceptance: new tables/APIs/UI gradually align to this operating-company model without breaking existing lead-centric behavior.

### Grand Unified Acceptance Test

- [~] Pick a city now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=pick_city` with market-opportunity city evidence.
- [~] Pick no vertical manually now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=pick_no_vertical_manually` with lead-evidence vertical key and vertical-pack match evidence.
- [~] Inspect market now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=inspect_market` with provenance, confidence intervals, and market sizing evidence.
- [~] Propose best business now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=propose_best_business` with launchability score, recommendation, and brand candidate evidence.
- [~] Approve launch now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=approve_launch` with operator launch approval and safe-to-launch gate blockers.
- [~] Create brand, site, phone, inbox, scripts, pricing, portal, payment path now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=create_brand_site_phone_inbox_scripts_pricing_portal_payment_path` tied to brand, owned-surface, communication, script, menu, and customer-room plans.
- [~] Acquire first customer.
- [~] Collect payment now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=collect_payment` with safe-to-charge, Stripe live-smoke, and invoice approval blockers.
- [~] Fulfill or route to qualified provider.
- [~] Prove completion.
- [~] Request review now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=request_review` with completed-service, review-policy, and no-incentive blockers.
- [~] Retain or upsell now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=retain_or_upsell` with retention-loop, cohort-history, and operator offer approval blockers.
- [~] Measure margin.
- [~] Decide scale/pause/shutdown and execute an approved local scale action.
- [~] Write postmortem now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=write_postmortem` with cohort-postmortem and learning-record requirements.
- [~] Improve itself now appears as `autonomousLaunchLoopPlan.stages[].key=improve_itself` with continual-learning, safe-PR, and regression-proofing gates.

Acceptance: repeat the flow across multiple verticals and markets. Current status: `readiness.autonomousLaunchLoopPlan` now records the full 16-stage local operating loop, `portfolio.autonomous_launch_loop_plans_grand_acceptance_flow` proves persisted readiness/channel/capability shape, snapshot propagation, and export serialization, and `portfolio.autonomous_launch_loop_replays_across_starter_verticals` proves local loop replay across cleaning in Austin, tutoring in Denver, and local SEO in Raleigh; repeated live execution across multiple real verticals and markets remains incomplete.

## Implementation Cycle 1: Portfolio Operating Model Foundation

Status: verified partial completion.

Changed files:
- `server/db.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `src/api.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `package.json`
- `README.md`
- `dist/index.html`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Durable SQLite tables for `organizations`, `workspaces`, `operators`, `territories`, `brands`, `market_opportunities`, `service_businesses`, `service_capabilities`, `vendor_partners`, `workflow_definitions`, and `portfolio_events`.
- `portfolioOperatingModel` access module with default bootstrap, territory creation, evidence-backed market opportunity persistence, service-business launch-candidate creation, default workflows, default capability normalization, vendor candidate persistence, event logging, and snapshot coverage.

## Implementation Cycle 17: Provider Dry-Run Receipts For Acquisition Actions

Status: verified partial completion.

Changed files:
- `server/db.js`
- `server/index.js`

- `src/views/PortfolioView.jsx`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`

Implemented:
- Added `portfolio_acquisition_action_receipts` as the provider-execution receipt ledger for acquisition actions.
- Execution now runs through provider-specific dry-run adapters for owned acquisition surfaces, local SEO pages, directory listings, outbound calls, outbound email, and generic channels.
- Receipts persist provider, mode, status, zero-cost posture, request payload, response payload, rollback payload, evidence, and timestamps.
- Live execution mode is fail-closed: it is blocked by action guardrails and, even with future guardrails, errors until a real live provider adapter is configured.
- Executed action proof now stores the provider receipt reference, channel state stores the latest receipt/provider, and rollback metadata stores provider rollback context.
- Snapshot rows now include `acquisitionActionReceipts` plus `providerReceipts` nested under acquisition action rows.
- Ops export includes provider dry-run receipt rows.
- Portfolio UI now shows a receipt count and provider/mode receipt text inside executed acquisition action rows.
- May Goals check now proves the receipt schema, dry-run owned-surface adapter, no external side effects, snapshot linkage, and export coverage.

## Implementation Cycle 18: Acquisition Action Rollback Compensation

Status: verified partial completion.

Changed files:
- `server/db.js`
- `server/index.js`

- `src/views/PortfolioView.jsx`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`

Implemented:
- Added `portfolioOperatingModel.rollbackAcquisitionAction()` for executed acquisition actions.
- Rollback restores the service-business channel from the saved rollback snapshot or removes the channel if it did not exist.
- Rollback writes a provider receipt with `status: rolled_back`, request/response/rollback/evidence payloads, zero cost, and no external side effects.
- The action moves from `executed` to `rolled_back` while preserving original execution proof and adding rollback receipt metadata.
- Service-business channel state records `lastRolledBackActionId`, `lastRollbackReceiptId`, and queue-level rollback metadata.
- Added `POST /api/portfolio/acquisition-actions/:id/rollback` plus a frontend API helper.
- Portfolio UI now shows a `Rollback` action for executed acquisition actions and a read-only rolled-back state after compensation.
- May Goals check now proves execute -> provider receipt -> rollback -> rollback receipt -> restored channel state -> snapshot/export coverage.

- Live preflight requires a validated dry-run receipt before it can even consider live execution.
- Preflight records provider-specific gates for live-spend guardrails, run mode, live side-effect flags, provider link status, rollback metadata, and whether the real live adapter is implemented.
- Preflight writes a durable provider receipt with `mode: live_preflight`, `status: blocked_live_preflight` or `ready_for_live`, request/response/rollback/evidence payloads, zero cost, and no external side effects.
- Action proof and rollback metadata now store the latest live preflight receipt, status, and blockers.
- Service-business channel state records live preflight status, receipt, blockers, and checked-at timestamp.
- Provider adapters now declare required provider links, side-effect flag families, and live implementation status for owned surfaces, local SEO, directory listings, calls, email, and generic channels.
- Portfolio UI now exposes `Preflight live` for executed actions and renders the blocker list.
- May Goals check now proves live preflight blocks honestly instead of pretending live provider work is ready.

Verification evidence:
- `npm run check:maygoals`: passed fourteen checks with live-preflight assertions.
- `npm run check`: passed over server and scripts.
- `npm run build`: passed with existing Vite large-chunk warnings.
- `npm run check:ops`: passed 53 ops/readiness/export checks.
- Rendered QA: Playwright verified desktop and mobile live-preflight behavior on `http://localhost:5200/`, after clicking `Approve + execute` and `Preflight live`, confirming blocked live-preflight text, live-adapter blocker text, two provider receipts, and no console/page errors. Screenshots: `/tmp/callan-acquisition-action-live-preflight-desktop.png`, `/tmp/callan-acquisition-action-live-preflight-mobile.png`.

- Live execution preserves the operator dry-run proof inside the dry-run receipt, records first-party side effects separately from external side effects, and keeps rollback tied to the original channel snapshot.
- Portfolio UI now exposes `Publish first-party` for executed actions after live preflight is ready, and launch surfaces render as clickable links.
- May Goals check now proves blocked preflight in mock mode, ready preflight in `demo_live` with builds enabled, live first-party publication, launch-surface retrieval, rollback, snapshot coverage, and redacted export coverage.

- Added deterministic `detectCompetitorWeaknesses` helper in `server/portfolio.js` that mines existing lead evidence (missing/weak website, weak/thin/missing online presence, missing phone, mobile/unknown phone classification, low presence confidence, missing source URL, uncertain callability) and emits per-weakness records with count/total/ratio/severity/evidence-lead-ids/exploit recommendation.
- `aggregateLeadMarketOpportunities` now persists detected weaknesses on `market_opportunities.signals.competitorWeaknesses`, a `competitorWeaknessSummary` (total/highSeverity/exploitableWeaknessRatio/topWeaknessKeys/source), and `risks.competitorWeaknessKeys`; high-severity weaknesses apply a small bounded score boost capped at +0.10 without bypassing existing confidence/evidence gates.
- Portfolio UI opportunity rows now render a `portfolio-tag-stack` chip list of the top weaknesses (`label · count/total · severity`) with the exploit recommendation as the chip title, reusing existing tag styling.
- `npm run check:maygoals` now asserts deterministic weakness extraction on the existing HVAC lead fixtures, that summary/risks fields are populated, that the snapshot exposes competitor weaknesses on market opportunities, and that the ops export surface includes the weakness payload via `signals_json`.

## Implementation Cycle 85: Market Demand Pressure And Service Urgency Classification

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/portfolio.js`
- `src/views/PortfolioView.jsx`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added a deterministic `detectServiceUrgency` helper in `server/portfolio.js` that classifies a city/vertical group into one of `emergency_first`, `urgent_response`, `planned_service`, or `discretionary` using a fixed urgency lexicon plus weighted class scoring against pack-derived text (valuePropHook, customerPersonaHint, pitchTone, marketSignals, growthPaths, retentionLoops, restricted claims, service offer) and lead-derived text (niche, business_name, callable_reason, source_url, online_presence_strength). The classifier emits `urgencyClass`, weighted `classScores`, raw lexicon `rawHits`, distinct `urgencyKeywords`, deterministic `responseExpectationMinutes`, `responseRequirements`, contributing `evidenceLeadIds`, pack-signal `packAlignment`, an exploit recommendation, and a `source` of `lead_evidence_service_urgency_detection`.
- Added `detectMarketDemandPressure` that combines urgency, competitor-weakness ratios, no-website overlap, callable responder gap, lead density, weak-presence majority, and pack growth-path alignment into a bounded `pressureScore` and `pressureLevel` (`low`/`moderate`/`elevated`/`high`) with per-driver weights, evidence, sources, an exploitation strategy, and the source-of-record inputs.
- `aggregateLeadMarketOpportunities` persists urgency and pressure under `signals.serviceUrgency` and `signals.demandPressure`, mirrors urgency class plus response requirements into `risks.serviceUrgencyClass`/`risks.serviceUrgencyResponseRequirements`, and mirrors pressure level plus driver keys into `risks.demandPressureLevel`/`risks.demandPressureDriverKeys`. A small bounded `pressureBoost` (capped at +0.06) nudges the launchability score without bypassing evidence/confidence gates.
- Portfolio UI opportunity rows now render a second `portfolio-tag-stack` chip line with `urgency · <class> · ≤<n>m`, `pressure · <level> · <score%>`, and the top demand-pressure driver keys, with the exploitation strategy and urgency exploit as chip titles.
- `npm run check:maygoals` now adds `portfolio.lead_evidence_classifies_service_urgency_and_demand_pressure` which asserts deterministic emergency_first classification on the existing Tempe HVAC fixture, response expectation of 60 minutes, presence of the after-hours coverage requirement, urgent-search-intent driver, exploitation strategy, and risks mirror. Snapshot assertions extend to verify `signals.serviceUrgency`/`signals.demandPressure`/`risks.serviceUrgencyClass`/`risks.demandPressureDriverKeys` on the Tempe opportunity, and the ops export assertions extend to require `serviceUrgency`, `emergency_first`, `after_hours_coverage_evidence`, `lead_evidence_service_urgency_detection`, `demandPressure`, `urgent_search_intent`, and `lead_evidence_demand_pressure_detection` strings in the serialized payload.

Verification evidence:
- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed with the new urgency/demand-pressure assertions on aggregation, snapshot, and export coverage.
- `npm run check` - passed `node --check` over `server/` and `scripts/`.
- `npm run build` - passed Vite build with existing large-chunk warnings.
- `git diff --check` - clean (no whitespace errors).
- Codex rerun verification: `npm run check:maygoals` passed 17 checks including `portfolio.lead_evidence_classifies_service_urgency_and_demand_pressure`; `npm run check`, `npm run build`, and `git diff --check` also passed locally after the Claude worker returned.
- Playwright/Vite smoke - started isolated mock dev server on API port 8805, seeded three Tempe emergency HVAC lead fixtures into the temp data dir, aggregated them, opened `http://localhost:5173/`, switched to the Portfolio tab, verified rendered chips `urgency - emergency first - <=60m`, `pressure - high - 100%`, and `urgent search intent`, captured `callan-portfolio-urgency-pressure-chips.png`, then stopped the dev server and verified ports 5173/8805 were clear.

## Implementation Cycle 86: TAM/SAM/Immediately-Callable Opportunity Estimates

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/portfolio.js`
- `src/views/PortfolioView.jsx`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added a deterministic `estimateMarketSizing` helper in `server/portfolio.js` that derives a market-sizing estimate purely from existing lead evidence and the chosen vertical pack's margin model. It computes `totalObservedLeads`, `immediatelyCallableLeads`, `weakPresenceCallableLeads` (lead-by-lead overlap of weak/missing/thin/no-website with callable/qualified/has-phone), `representativeTicketCents`, `estimatedFulfillmentCostCents`, `contributionMarginCents` (ticket − fulfillment when known), `maxAcquisitionCostCents` (from `pack.marginModel.maxAcquisitionCostPct`), `targetGrossMarginPct`, `estimatedCallableRevenueCents` (immediately callable × ticket), `weakPresenceCallableRevenueCents`, `serviceableAvailableMarketCents` (SAM proxy = immediately callable × ticket), and a `firstWaveConversionRate` derived from the urgency class base rate (`emergency_first` 0.40, `urgent_response` 0.28, `planned_service` 0.18, `discretionary` 0.10) plus bounded boosts from demand pressureScore, exploitable competitor-weakness ratio, and average research confidence. From that it derives `obtainableFirstWaveLeads`, `obtainableFirstWaveCents` (SOM proxy), and `obtainableFirstWaveMarginCents`. Evidence is preserved through `evidenceLeadIds`, `inputs`, and structured `assumptions`, and the result is tagged `source: lead_evidence_market_sizing_estimate`. No live scraping, model calls, or external surveys are invoked.
- `aggregateLeadMarketOpportunities` persists the estimate on `signals.marketSizing` and mirrors the full payload onto `unit_economics.marketSizing` so finance/launch surfaces can read it without re-deriving. Key fields (`marketSizingConfidence`, `obtainableFirstWaveCents`, `serviceableAvailableMarketCents`) also mirror onto `risks` for downstream readiness/operator visibility.
- Portfolio UI opportunity rows render a new compact tag stack with `callable · <N>/<total>`, `weak · <N>`, `SAM · $X`, `SOM · $X` (with conversion-rate-aware tooltip), and `est confidence · X%`. Tooltips expose assumptions and SOM derivation.
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js` adds `portfolio.lead_evidence_estimates_tam_sam_immediately_callable_opportunity` which asserts on the existing Tempe HVAC fixture that `signals.marketSizing` reports 3/3 observed/callable/weak-presence-callable leads, a 60000 cent representative ticket, a 180000 cent SAM proxy, a positive sub-1 first-wave conversion, a SOM proxy that respects `obtainable = round(weak × rate)` and `cents = obtainable × ticket`, an emergency_first urgency input, at least four structured assumptions including the conversion derivation and the no-live-scraping note, evidence lead IDs that all start with `lead:`, a confidence in (0,1], the `unit_economics.marketSizing` mirror, and the `risks` mirrors. The snapshot assertion block requires the same `signals.marketSizing`/`unit_economics.marketSizing`/`risks.marketSizingConfidence`/`risks.serviceableAvailableMarketCents` propagation on the Tempe opportunity. The ops-export serialization assertion requires `marketSizing`, `lead_evidence_market_sizing_estimate`, `serviceableAvailableMarketCents`, `obtainableFirstWaveCents`, `firstWaveConversionRate`, `weakPresenceCallableLeads`, and `immediatelyCallableLeads` strings in the redacted export payload.

Verification evidence:
- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed all 18 checks including the new `portfolio.lead_evidence_estimates_tam_sam_immediately_callable_opportunity`, snapshot mirror assertions, and the expanded export-serialization assertions.
- `npm run check` - passed `node --check` over `server/` and `scripts/`.
- `npm run build` - passed Vite build with existing large-chunk warnings (no new warnings introduced).
- `git diff --check` - clean (no whitespace errors).
- Codex rerun verification: `npm run check:maygoals` passed 18 checks including the new market-sizing check; `npm run check`, `npm run build`, and `git diff --check` also passed locally after the Claude worker returned.
- Playwright/Vite smoke - started isolated mock dev server on API port 8806, seeded three Tempe emergency HVAC lead fixtures into the temp data dir, aggregated them, opened `http://localhost:5173/`, switched to the Portfolio tab, verified rendered market-sizing chips `callable - 3/3`, `SAM - $1,800`, `SOM - $1,800`, and `est confidence - 79%`, captured `callan-portfolio-market-sizing-chips.png`, then stopped the dev server and verified ports 5173/8806 were clear.

## Implementation Cycle 87: Owner Responsiveness Prediction From Lead Evidence

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/portfolio.js`
- `src/views/PortfolioView.jsx`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added a deterministic `predictOwnerResponsiveness` helper in `server/portfolio.js` that runs purely off existing lead fields (`risk_status`, `phone`, `phone_classification`, `callable_reason`, `website`, `source_url`, `presence_confidence`, `online_presence_strength`, `niche`, `business_name`) plus the urgency/demand-pressure context. The classifier resolves a `responsivenessClass` of `responsive_likely`, `mixed`, `unresponsive_risk`, or `evidence_insufficient` via fixed thresholds on `callableCoverageRatio`, `businessPhoneCoverageRatio`, and a bounded `responseFrictionScore`. The friction score combines weighted negative regex matches (`no_callback`, `missed_call`, `unanswered`, `no_response`, `slow_response`), weighted positive matches (`fast_response`, `callback_path`, `response_time_promise`, `business_landline_evidence`), coverage penalty (callable/business-phone gaps + weak-presence + low-confidence ratios), coverage bonus (callable + business phone share), and an urgency penalty when emergency/urgent demand collides with low callable coverage. The function emits `recommendedAcquisitionMotion` (`call_ready` / `inbound_first` / `proof_first` / `evidence_review`), `blockers`/`evidenceRequired` (callable-reason text, research confidence, source URL, phone number), per-pattern `evidenceLeadIdsByKey`, top `evidenceLeadIds`, deterministic `assumptions`, structured `inputs` and `weights`, an `exploit` narrative aligned to the resolved class, and `source: lead_evidence_owner_responsiveness_prediction`. The helper is exported so deterministic checks can run against synthetic friction fixtures without DB state.
- `aggregateLeadMarketOpportunities` calls `predictOwnerResponsiveness` after urgency and demand pressure are known, persists the full payload on `signals.ownerResponsiveness`, and mirrors `risks.ownerResponsivenessClass`, `risks.recommendedAcquisitionMotion`, `risks.responseFrictionScore`, and `risks.ownerResponsivenessBlockers` for downstream readiness/operator visibility. The launchability score is not boosted off responsiveness — it informs motion choice and evidence requirements rather than gating launch decisions directly.
- Portfolio UI opportunity rows now render a compact owner-responsiveness chip stack with `owners · <class>`, `motion · <recommended motion>`, `friction · <N>%`, `callable · <N>%`, and the first two blockers as `needs · <blocker>` chips. The exploit narrative and motion key appear as chip titles, so the row stays compact while still surfacing the deterministic recommendation and missing-evidence asks.
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js` adds `portfolio.lead_evidence_predicts_owner_responsiveness_and_recommended_motion`. On the Tempe HVAC fixture it asserts `responsivenessClass=responsive_likely`, `recommendedAcquisitionMotion=call_ready`, `callableCoverageRatio=1`, `businessPhoneCoverageRatio=1`, `phonePresentRatio=1`, `responseFrictionScore ≤ 0.38`, no negative signals, the `business_landline_evidence` positive signal, three lead-prefixed evidence IDs, at least four assumptions including the no-live-calls assertion, urgency input class `emergency_first`, exploit narrative containing "responsive", and the four `risks` mirrors. It then exercises `predictOwnerResponsiveness` directly with a synthetic friction fixture (no callback, missed calls, unanswered, voicemail full, mobile_risk phones, low confidence, no source URL) to prove `unresponsive_risk` + `proof_first` + `responseFrictionScore ≥ 0.65` + matched negative keys (`no_callback`, `missed_call`, `unanswered`) + blockers (`phone_number_evidence_required`, `research_confidence_required`) + a proof-first exploit narrative, and finally calls the helper with an empty lead array to prove `evidence_insufficient` + `evidence_review` + a `callable_reason_text_required` blocker. The snapshot assertion block additionally requires `signals.ownerResponsiveness` and the responsiveness `risks` mirrors on the Tempe opportunity, and the ops-export serialization assertion requires `ownerResponsiveness`, `responsive_likely`, `call_ready`, `recommendedAcquisitionMotion`, `responseFrictionScore`, `lead_evidence_owner_responsiveness_prediction`, `callableCoverageRatio`, and `businessPhoneCoverageRatio` strings in the redacted export payload.

Verification evidence:
- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed all 19 checks including the new `portfolio.lead_evidence_predicts_owner_responsiveness_and_recommended_motion`, the snapshot mirror assertions, and the expanded export-serialization assertions.
- `npm run check` - passed `node --check` over `server/` and `scripts/`.
- `npm run build` - passed Vite build with existing large-chunk warnings (no new warnings introduced).
- `git diff --check` - clean (no whitespace errors).
- Codex rerun verification: `npm run check:maygoals` passed 19 checks including the owner-responsiveness prediction check; `npm run check`, `npm run build`, and `git diff --check` also passed locally after the Claude worker returned.
- Playwright/Vite smoke - started isolated mock dev server on API port 8807, seeded three Tempe emergency HVAC owner-responsiveness fixtures into the temp data dir, aggregated them, opened `http://localhost:5173/`, switched to the Portfolio tab, verified rendered owner chips `owners - responsive likely`, `motion - call ready`, `friction - 0%`, and `callable - 100%`, captured `callan-portfolio-owner-responsiveness-chips.png`, then stopped the dev server and verified ports 5173/8807 were clear.

## Implementation Cycle 90: Automatic First Acquisition Motion From Market Recommendation

Status: implemented and verified locally.

- `server/db.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`

Implemented:
- `planLaunchFromMarketOpportunity` now defaults `autoLaunchFirstAcquisitionMotion` to true. For a new or existing service-business launch candidate it refreshes the portfolio acquisition strategy, selects the zero-attempt `launch_first_motion` recommendation for `owned_acquisition_surface`, approves the local dry-run scope under `market-recommendation-autopilot`, executes the dry-run with `owned_surface_builder`, records a provider receipt, and returns a `firstAcquisitionMotion` payload with strategy/action/receipt/service-business evidence.
- The helper is idempotent: if the first motion already executed, it returns `reason: already_executed` and does not mint duplicate provider receipts.
- `normalizePortfolioAcquisitionAction` now marks this first motion with `automaticDryRunAllowed: true`, `autoExecutionScope: local_dry_run_only`, and `operatorReviewRequiredBeforeLive: true`, while preserving `liveExternalSpendAllowed: false` and rollback/proof guardrails.
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js` now includes `portfolio.market_recommendation_auto_launches_first_acquisition_motion`, proving the automatic dry-run action, zero external side effects, owned-surface provider receipt, launch-ready service-business channel state, action queue state, and idempotent repeat planning. Snapshot/export count assertions now account for the additional strategy recommendation, learning record, action, and receipt.
- `README.md` documents that market recommendation launch planning now starts the first acquisition motion automatically while keeping live/public mutations and spend gated.

- Added `recordMarketRecommendationOutcome()` in `server/portfolio.js` plus `POST /api/portfolio/market-opportunities/:id/record-outcome` and `api.recordPortfolioMarketOutcome()`. Operators or checks can now record `false_positive`, `market_failure`, `launch_rejected`, or `unprofitable_test` outcomes against a market opportunity with reason keys and evidence.
- Recording a negative outcome appends `signals.marketOutcomeLearning`, writes a durable `portfolio_learning_records` row with `source_type: market_opportunity_outcome`, moves the opportunity to `learning_review`, mirrors `risks.marketOutcomeLearningApplied`, `risks.marketFalsePositiveCount`, `risks.marketFailureReasonKeys`, `risks.marketRecommendationPenalty`, and `risks.learnedDecisionOverride`, and downgrades repeat launch decisions to `watch` or `avoid`.
- `aggregateLeadMarketOpportunities()` now detects existing `signals.marketOutcomeLearning` for the same city/vertical opportunity, preserves fresh source evidence, reapplies the learned score penalty, and records `appliedToFreshEvidence`, pre/post learning scores, and the decision override before persisting the refreshed opportunity.
- Portfolio rows render `market outcome learning` chips (`learned`, `penalty`, `next`, reason keys) and include a `Mark false positive` button that posts the outcome and refreshes the snapshot. README documents the local false-positive learning loop.
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js` adds `portfolio.market_false_positive_learning_reduces_repeat_launch_recommendation`, proving outcome recording, durable learning record creation, risk mirrors, opportunity downgrade to `watch`, repeat aggregation penalty application, snapshot propagation, and ops export serialization.
