- Portfolio opportunity rows render compact `market recommendation provenance` chips (`why`, `explained`, `proof`, and top `reason` values). README documents the provenance ledger and the fact that it does not invoke live scraping, provider lookups, ad platforms, or external feeds.
- Fixed the market false-positive learning path so provenance decision/score update when a recommendation is downgraded to `watch`/`avoid`; the ledger no longer preserves stale `launch_candidate` reasoning after learning is applied.
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js` adds `portfolio.market_recommendation_has_source_provenance`, proving source-model coverage, top reason keys, evidence IDs/source URLs, risk mirrors, confidence interval linkage, snapshot propagation, export serialization, and the learning consistency path.

- Added local bulk-review recording for assignment queues. Bulk review rejects live mode, records reviewed item counts, leaves inbox rows unresolved, updates the queue's last receipt, and creates a proposed `operator_handoff_eval` learning record.
- Added API routes and frontend helpers for listing role queues and recording operator bulk reviews.
- Extended snapshots, ops export, tenant-isolation scope coverage, and Portfolio launch-candidate chips with assignment queue count and bulk-review receipt count.
- MayGoals verification now proves queue grouping, local bulk-review receipt creation, proposed eval feedback, live-mode rejection, snapshot/export serialization, and no live provider/acquisition side effects.

- Operator inbox claims now record lease expiry in assignment evidence and action receipt response, with a bounded default lease duration.
- Added local-only `portfolioOperatorInbox.expireLeases()` plus an API helper/route. Expiry rejects live mode, emits `expire_claim` action receipts, moves active assignments to `expired`, does not resolve blockers, and does not message customers, mutate providers, publish externally, mutate staffing, or perform live side effects.
- Claiming an item with an expired active lease now expires the stale claim before allowing a new local claim.
- Snapshots and Portfolio rows now expose expired-claim counts alongside claimed counts.
- MayGoals verification now proves lease duration recording, live expiry rejection, lease expiry receipts, reclaim after expiry, release after reclaim, snapshot/export serialization, and no live provider/acquisition side effects.

## Implementation Cycle 151: GitHub Check-Run Observation Receipts

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Implemented:
- Added `observeGithubCheckRuns()` and `normalizeGithubCheckRunObservation()` in `server/evalAdapters.js`. With no `GITHUB_TOKEN`, the adapter returns a deterministic `sandbox_fixture` payload with four successful check runs and explicitly keeps `githubApiCalled`, live GitHub observation, external PR verification, merge permission, mutation, and live side effects false. With a token, it performs read-only GitHub Pulls and check-runs GETs, then still keeps mutation and merge blocked.
- Added durable `portfolio_eval_github_check_run_observation_receipts` chained after GitHub PR observation adapter-contract receipts. The receipt records workspace/service/GitHub-observation/preflight/external-CI/PR-verification/submitted-PR/merge-approval/PR-open/proposal/promotion/workflow/learning-record scope, observation mode, check-run counts/conclusion, GitHub API flags, external-verification flags, request/response/evidence JSON, indexes, tenant-isolation scope, snapshot counts/lists, service-business counts, coverage-gap detection, and operations export coverage.
- Added `portfolioOperatorInbox.recordEvalGithubCheckRunObservation()`, `getEvalGithubCheckRunObservationReceipt()`, and `listEvalGithubCheckRunObservationReceipts()`. The recorder rejects live mutation modes, requires the blocked PR-observation adapter contract, prevents sandbox evidence from claiming live GitHub observation or external PR verification, idempotently keys on `(github_pr_observation_receipt_id, observation_kind)`, updates learning-record eval metadata, emits a portfolio event, and keeps operator merge approval and merge execution blocked.
- Added `POST /api/portfolio/eval-github-pr-observation-receipts/:id/check-run-observation`, API client wiring, and Portfolio operator desk controls/visibility for `GitHub check-runs`, `GitHub check-runs observed`, `Eval GitHub Check Runs`, `GitHub check-run observation recorded`, `sandbox fixture observed`, `check-run conclusion success`, `GitHub API observation false`, `github API called false`, `live GitHub observation false`, `pull request externally verified false`, and `merge allowed false`.

The check also covers live-mode rejection, missing parent IDs, invalid observation results, idempotent duplicate recording, snapshot counts, service-business counts, export counts, learning-record metadata, serialized export tokens, and UI source tokens.
- README now documents GitHub check-run observation as the read-only receipt after PR observation, including sandbox fallback behavior without `GITHUB_TOKEN` and the Portfolio `GitHub-check-runs` operator control.

## Implementation Cycle 153: Safe-to-Renew Subscription Aftercare Gate

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/safeToRenew.js`
- `server/safeToSell.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `src/views/OperationsView.jsx`
- `scripts/ops-check.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added `buildSafeToRenewStatus()` as a read-only renewal inspector for active/trialing/past-due hosting and edit-care subscriptions. It queries subscription rows and `hosting_subscription_status` account-manager tasks, reports active MRR, task/proof counts, missing/due/stale/blocked renewal blockers, next actions, and explicitly records that the check sent no renewal message and created no live side effects.
- Wired safe-to-renew into safe-to-sell reports, blockers, next actions, decision receipts, compact durable receipt history, and CLI printing. A safe-to-sell report now fails closed when active renewal subscriptions lack account-manager coverage, fresh dry-run proof, or operator review for past-due status.
- Exposed renewal status in `/api/ops/command-center` inline and snapshot summaries, including renewal blockers for stale snapshots that predate the new lane.
- Added Operations command-center UI metrics for renewal readiness and renewal proof coverage without adding new live actions.
- Added deterministic regression coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js` and `scripts/maygoals-check.js`, proving an active subscription blocks while missing the hosting task, blocks while due without dry-run proof, becomes safe after a dry-run preview, stays read-only, and propagates into safe-to-sell decision receipts.
- Documented the safe-to-renew lane in README, including the explicit no-email/no-Stripe/no-subscription-mutation boundary.
- Stabilized an existing ops-check timing flake in the provider-incident regression by ensuring the synthetic incident timestamp is newer than prior successful live smoke and cleared afterward with a later success event.

Verification evidence:
- `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p ...` - offloaded a read-only implementation scout as requested. The scout confirmed the account-manager/subscription surfaces, read-only boundary, command-center placement, and verification commands. A later Claude verification worker hung without output and was terminated; direct verification below completed successfully.
- `node --check server/safeToRenew.js`, `node --check server/safeToSell.js`, `node --check server/index.js`, `node --check scripts/ops-check.js`, and `node --check scripts/maygoals-check.js` - passed. `node --check src/views/OperationsView.jsx` is not supported by Node's plain syntax checker because `.jsx` has no native loader; Vite build covered it instead.

Implemented:
- Added durable `safe_to_renew_reports` snapshots with generated-at/status/command/count indexes, redacted JSON report storage, latest/list/summary helpers, and durable decision-receipt style metadata for renewal readiness.
- Expanded `server/safeToRenew.js` from a read-only inline inspector into a standalone self-check lane with `SAFE_TO_RENEW_JOB_TYPE`, snapshot freshness evaluation, next-action synthesis, durable report recording, decision-receipt proof, compact receipt history, durable queue enqueueing, boot/interval scheduler support, and human-readable CLI printing.
- Added `SAFE_TO_RENEW_SELF_CHECK_ENABLED` and `SAFE_TO_RENEW_SELF_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS` env controls, wired the self-check into the durable job handler map, server startup scheduler, ops observability, operations export, recurring-maintenance health, stale-recurring-job refresh, and `/api/ops/self-check` scope routing.
- Added `npm run safe-to-renew` as a standalone fail-closed renewal/aftercare gate. The command supports `--report-only` and `--no-record`, emits JSON plus a compact operator summary, records snapshots by default, and stays read-only with no email, Stripe, subscription, or customer-message mutations.
- Upgraded the Operations command center to prefer durable renewal snapshots when present, expose recent safe-to-renew receipts, and add a scoped `renew` self-check operator action without introducing any live renewal send path.
- Extended deterministic regression coverage so May Goals and ops checks prove durable safe-to-renew snapshots, duplicate enqueue idempotency, snapshot freshness/staleness, durable receipt proof, no live side effects, scheduler-health exposure, export coverage, and safe-to-renew history in observability.
- Updated README and CLAUDE command guidance so future agents can discover the standalone renewal gate instead of treating renewal readiness as only a nested safe-to-sell detail.

The action requires a subscription-owned renewal playbook, records a `renewal_plan_reviewed` portal action, writes an inbound `customer_renewal_plan_reviewed` contact event, emits an SSE event, and is idempotent per playbook.

- Extended `scripts/maygoals-check.js` so the safe-to-renew acceptance path also proves a customer-visible portal renewal review can be recorded and surfaced without live side effects.
- Documented the new portal renewal review API/state boundary in README and added `npm run check:portal` to CLAUDE command guidance.

- Offloaded the first implementation pass to `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p ...` as requested. The Claude worker stayed silent and had to be killed after several minutes, but it produced the core renewal change-request patch before termination; the patch was then reviewed, repaired, and verified directly.
- Added token-scoped `requestRenewalChange()` in `server/customerPortal.js` plus `POST /api/share/build/:token/renewal/change-request`. The action requires a lead-owned subscription with a latest safe-to-renew playbook, records a `renewal_change_requested` portal action, writes an inbound `customer_renewal_change_requested` contact event, emits an SSE event, and dedupes exact same playbook/type/note submissions while allowing distinct customer requests.
- Extended portal `subscriptionManagement` with aggregate change-request counts, subscriptions-with-change-request counts, per-subscription change request rows, latest request state, and explicit proof that the portal intake creates no live side effects.
- Added the customer-facing renewal change request control to the share portal subscription card: customers can submit a brief note, see per-subscription pending-request counts, and see aggregate safety copy that no automatic billing changes are made from the portal.
- Preserved the safety boundary end-to-end: renewal change requests send no email/SMS/calls, create no portal broadcast, create no checkout/payment link, apply no discount, change no live price, mutate no Stripe state, and mutate no subscription rows.
- Extended `scripts/portal-check.js` with two distinct renewal change requests, duplicate-request reuse, inbound contact event assertions, subscription-row immutability checks, portal aggregate/per-subscription state checks, and no-live-side-effect proofs.
- Extended `scripts/maygoals-check.js` so `company.safe_to_renew_status_tracks_subscription_aftercare` now proves customer-visible renewal review and customer-visible renewal change request in the same safe-to-renew acceptance path.
- Updated README and CLAUDE command guidance for the renewal review/change-request portal boundary.
- Fixed a browser-discovered share-portal layout regression: `.nyna-share` had a fixed fifth grid row that caused the new subscription section to overflow into the footer. The grid now uses auto rows so optional aftercare/subscription sections expand normally.

Verification evidence:

- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed all 86 checks, including the new renewal change-request assertions inside `company.safe_to_renew_status_tracks_subscription_aftercare`.

## Implementation Cycle 162: Guarded Renewal Customer-Message Execution Gate

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/customerPortal.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `src/views/OperationsView.jsx`
- `scripts/ops-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added `executeRenewalCustomerMessagePreflight()` for the next step after renewal customer-message proof packets. It accepts only `renewal_customer_message_preflight` actions and creates durable `renewal_customer_message_send_receipt` rows instead of mutating the subscription by default.
- Added a strict live-execution safety gate for renewal customer messages. The send path blocks unless live execution is explicitly requested, operator approval is true, message copy is approved, customer consent is documented, the target email passes the email side-effect gate, AgentMail config is present, and a fresh successful live AgentMail smoke result exists.
- Added a fail-closed blocked receipt path with internal contact-event and ops SSE evidence. Local/mock runs record why sending was blocked and explicitly report no email, provider, subscription, Stripe, payment, price, discount, checkout, or portal-broadcast side effects.
- Added the live AgentMail execution path behind the gate. If all prerequisites pass, it calls `sendAgentMailMessage()`, records a `sent` receipt and outbound contact event; provider failures record provider runtime incidents and `failed_provider` receipts.
- Added admin-protected `POST /api/ops/renewal-customer-message-preflights/:id/execute`, returning fail-closed 400/404 errors for invalid preflight IDs and blocked execution conditions.
- Added `summarizeRenewalCustomerMessageSendReceiptQueue()` and surfaced send receipts in `opsObservability()`, `/api/ops/command-center`, and `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalCustomerMessageSendReceipts`.
- Added an Operations command-center `send gate` metric so operators can see blocked/sent/failed renewal message execution receipts separately from proof packets.
- Added `safe_to_renew.renewal_customer_message_execution_gate_fails_closed_without_live_prereqs` coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js`: the test seeds a renewal change request and message preflight, clears AgentMail smoke rows, proves local execution blocks with all expected prerequisites, proves idempotency, verifies queue/observability/export evidence, and asserts there are no live side effects or subscription/payment mutations.
- Documented the execution route, live AgentMail prerequisites, local fail-closed behavior, and Operations send-gate visibility in README and updated CLAUDE command guidance.

Verification evidence:
- `node --check server/customerPortal.js`, `node --check server/index.js`, `node --check server/ops.js`, and `node --check scripts/ops-check.js` - passed.
- `npm run check:ops` - passed, including `safe_to_renew.renewal_customer_message_execution_gate_fails_closed_without_live_prereqs`.
- `npm run check:portal` - passed.
- `npm run check:ci` - passed, covering syntax check, production evals, handoff assertions, adapter contract, and all May Goals checks.
- `npm run build` - passed with the existing Vite large-chunk warning. Generated `dist/index.html` asset-hash churn from the build was restored so no generated artifact noise remained.
- `git diff --check` - passed.

## Implementation Cycle 163: Guarded Renewal Billing-Change Execution Gate and Sticky-Blocker Repair

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/providers/stripe.js`
- `server/customerPortal.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `src/views/OperationsView.jsx`
- `scripts/ops-check.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Offloaded a sidecar audit/scouting pass to `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p ...` as requested. Claude reported two concrete customer-message send-gate defects: blocked receipts could stick after live-smoke/config blockers changed, and provider failures did not emit contact/SSE evidence. Both were repaired before closing this cycle, and the billing execution gate was selected as the next slice.
- Added `applyStripeSubscriptionChange()` in `server/providers/stripe.js`, a narrow live Stripe adapter for operator-approved renewal subscription changes. It supports cancel-at-period-end, pause, resume, and target Stripe price changes through `stripe.subscriptions.update()` with idempotency keys and normalized receipt data.
- Added `executeRenewalBillingChangePreflight()` for `renewal_billing_change_preflight` actions. It records durable `renewal_billing_change_execution_receipt` rows and refuses to call Stripe unless live execution is explicit, operator approval is present, the billing change is approved, customer consent is documented, pricing-policy review is present for price-sensitive changes, the subscription has a Stripe subscription ID, `LIVE_PAYMENTS`/`RUN_MODE` permits payment side effects, Stripe config is present, and a fresh live Stripe smoke receipt exists.
- Added blocked, provider-failed, and applied billing execution receipt paths. Blocked local receipts record internal contact-event and ops SSE evidence with all live side effects false. Provider failures record runtime incidents plus failure contact/SSE evidence. Applied receipts update local subscription state only after Stripe returns success and mark `subscriptionChanged`, `stripeStateChanged`, `providerMutationPerformed`, and `liveSideEffects` truthfully.
- Added admin-protected `POST /api/ops/renewal-billing-change-preflights/:id/execute` with the same fail-closed 400/404/500 envelope as the other renewal operator routes.
- Added `summarizeRenewalBillingExecutionReceiptQueue()` and surfaced billing execution receipts through `opsObservability()`, `/api/ops/command-center`, and `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalBillingExecutionReceipts`.
- Added an Operations command-center `bill gate` metric showing applied/blocked billing execution receipts beside the message send gate.
- Repaired sticky blocked-receipt dedupe for both renewal customer-message sends and renewal billing executions: previously blocked receipts are reused only when the current blocker set is unchanged; changed blocker state creates fresh evidence instead of trapping the operator on stale failure.
- Added internal contact-event and SSE observability for AgentMail customer-message provider failures, plus explicit metadata safety flags for successful sends.
- Added `safe_to_renew.subscription_billing_change_execution_gate_fails_closed_without_live_prereqs` coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js`. The test proves default/local execution blocks with expected Stripe prerequisites, is idempotent for unchanged blockers, creates fresh receipts when live-smoke/config blockers change, surfaces queue/observability/export evidence, rejects wrong action types, and leaves subscriptions, payments, Stripe IDs, prices, and outbound customer messages untouched.
- Extended `safe_to_renew.renewal_customer_message_execution_gate_fails_closed_without_live_prereqs` to prove changed AgentMail blocker state creates fresh receipts rather than reusing stale blocked evidence.
- Fixed two time-brittle May Goals assertions in `scripts/maygoals-check.js`: duplicate operator claim now uses the same fixed lease window, and SLA staffing analytics asserts substantial overdue pressure without requiring every open item to be overdue.
- Documented the billing execution route, live Stripe prerequisites, local fail-closed behavior, and Operations `bill gate` visibility in README and updated CLAUDE command guidance.

Verification evidence:
- `node --check server/providers/stripe.js`, `node --check server/customerPortal.js`, `node --check server/index.js`, `node --check server/ops.js`, and `node --check scripts/ops-check.js` - passed.
- `npm run check:ops` - passed, including `safe_to_renew.subscription_billing_change_execution_gate_fails_closed_without_live_prereqs` and the refreshed sticky-blocker assertions.
- `npm run check:portal` - passed.
- `npm run check:maygoals` - initially exposed existing time-brittle operator-inbox assertions; after pinning/relaxing the unsafe assertions it passed all May Goals checks.
- `npm run check:ci` - initially failed only because of the same May Goals time-brittle assertion; after repair it passed syntax check, production evals, handoff assertions, adapter contract, and all May Goals checks.
- `npm run build` - passed with the existing Vite large-chunk warning. Generated `dist/index.html` asset-hash churn from the build was restored so no generated artifact noise remained.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `git status --short dist node_modules/.vite/deps/_metadata.json` - returned no output.

## Implementation Cycle 164: Customer-Visible Renewal Confirmation Receipts

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/customerPortal.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `src/views/OperationsView.jsx`
- `scripts/ops-check.js`
- `scripts/portal-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Used `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p ...` as a bounded sidecar review for this slice. Claude flagged concrete edge cases around dedupe, mismatched receipt pairing, lead binding, and flattened source flags; those were folded into the final implementation and tests.
- Added `createRenewalCustomerConfirmationReceipt()` for customer-visible `renewal_customer_confirmation_receipt` rows. A confirmation can be recorded only from an `applied` renewal billing execution receipt and/or a `sent` renewal customer-message receipt, with optional lead binding, same-lead/same-subscription checks, same renewal change-request/playbook checks when both sources are present, and source-id-only dedupe so summary wording cannot create duplicate confirmations.
- Confirmation receipts expose source truth without creating new work: `customerVisible`, `portalVisible`, billing/message source IDs, `subscriptionChanged`, `stripeStateChanged`, `customerMessageSent`, `paymentLinkCreated`, `checkoutLinkCreated`, `discountApplied`, `priceChanged`, provider mutation/source-live-side-effect flags, and explicit `confirmationMessageSent:false`, `portalBroadcastSent:false`, and `confirmationLiveSideEffects:false`.
- Added admin-protected `POST /api/ops/renewal-confirmations`, with fail-closed 400/404 responses for invalid status, mismatched action type, unknown receipt IDs, wrong lead binding, or unrelated source receipts.
- Added `summarizeRenewalCustomerConfirmationQueue()` and surfaced confirmation receipts through `opsObservability()`, `/api/ops/command-center`, `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalCustomerConfirmationReceipts`, and an Operations command-center `confirm` metric.
- Extended customer portal subscription state so `subscriptionManagement` and each subscription row expose confirmation counts, latest confirmation, source side-effect flags, and the customer-visible confirmation list without leaking raw portal-action internals.
- Repaired the ops-check harness posture leak discovered during verification: production-readiness tests left dummy live provider keys on `env`, so `safe_to_sell.preflight_refreshes_stale_ops_maintenance` could accidentally try live Supermemory and time out. The check now resets to explicit mock posture before that mock maintenance preflight.
- Added `safe_to_renew.customer_visible_confirmation_requires_applied_or_sent_sources` coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js`: it proves blocked billing receipts cannot be confirmed, applied/sent receipts create a customer-visible confirmation, source IDs are paired to the same request/lead, dedupe is source-based rather than summary-based, ops/export/queue/portal surfaces include the receipt, source payment/checkout flags propagate, and no subscription, Stripe price, or outbound customer message is mutated by the confirmation itself.
- Extended `scripts/portal-check.js` so token-scoped portal state proves confirmed source effects appear in the customer subscription-management surface while the confirmation itself sends no additional message or portal broadcast.
- Documented the confirmation route and customer-visible/no-new-side-effects boundary in README and updated CLAUDE command guidance.

Verification evidence:
- `node --check server/customerPortal.js`, `node --check server/index.js`, `node --check server/ops.js`, `node --check scripts/ops-check.js`, and `node --check scripts/portal-check.js` - passed.
- `npm run check:portal` - passed, including the portal renewal confirmation assertions.
- `npm run check:ops` - first exposed the mock/posture leak above after the new confirmation check had passed; after the harness repair, a fresh rerun passed, including `safe_to_renew.customer_visible_confirmation_requires_applied_or_sent_sources`.
- `npm run check:ci` - passed, covering syntax check, production evals, handoff assertions, adapter contract, and all May Goals checks.
- `npm run build` - passed with the existing Vite large-chunk warning. Generated `dist/index.html` asset-hash churn from the build was restored so no generated artifact noise remained.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `git status --short dist node_modules/.vite/deps/_metadata.json` - returned no output.

## Implementation Cycle 165: Customer Renewal Confirmation Acknowledgements

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/customerPortal.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `src/views/OperationsView.jsx`
- `src/views/ShareView.jsx`
- `src/styles.css`
- `scripts/ops-check.js`
- `scripts/portal-check.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added `acknowledgeRenewalCustomerConfirmation()` for token-scoped customer acknowledgement of visible renewal confirmation receipts. It accepts only `renewal_customer_confirmation_receipt` rows owned by the portal lead and only while they are `visible_to_customer`.
- Added durable `renewal_customer_confirmation_acknowledged` portal-action receipts with inbound contact-event and SSE evidence. The acknowledgement records source confirmation/subscription/billing/message IDs plus `customerAcknowledged:true`, while proving `customerMessageSent:false`, `subscriptionChanged:false`, `stripeStateChanged:false`, `paymentLinkCreated:false`, `checkoutLinkCreated:false`, `portalBroadcastSent:false`, and `confirmationAcknowledgementLiveSideEffects:false`.
- Made acknowledgement idempotent per confirmation ID so repeated customer clicks reuse the existing receipt instead of creating duplicate acknowledgements.
- Added token route `POST /api/share/build/:token/renewal/confirmations/:confirmationId/acknowledge`, with fail-closed 400/404 behavior for wrong action types, missing confirmation rows, mismatched portal leads, or non-visible confirmations.
- Added `summarizeRenewalCustomerConfirmationAcknowledgementQueue()` and surfaced acknowledgement receipts through `opsObservability()`, `/api/ops/command-center`, `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalCustomerConfirmationAcknowledgements`, and an Operations command-center `ack` metric.
- Extended portal subscription-management state so top-level and per-subscription rows expose acknowledgement counts, latest acknowledgement, acknowledged confirmation rows, and no-side-effect safety flags.
- Added the Share portal UI for customer-visible renewal confirmations: each confirmation row shows its summary and a `mark received` action that becomes a disabled `received` state after acknowledgement, plus success/error messaging.
- Added compact confirmation-list styling that fits inside the existing aftercare/subscription surface without introducing a new page section or leaking internal portal-action JSON.
- Extended `safe_to_renew.customer_visible_confirmation_requires_applied_or_sent_sources` coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js` to prove acknowledgement creation, idempotency, queue/observability/export/portal visibility, no outbound customer messages, and no subscription/payment/Stripe mutations.
- Extended `scripts/portal-check.js` so token-scoped portal state proves confirmations can be acknowledged by customers and then render as acknowledged.
- Fixed another May Goals time-brittle staffing analytics setup by pinning unresolved operator inbox item timestamps before the local-only SLA analytics assertion.
- Documented the customer acknowledgement route and no-new-side-effects boundary in README and updated CLAUDE command guidance.

Verification evidence:
- `node --check server/customerPortal.js`, `node --check server/index.js`, `node --check server/ops.js`, `node --check scripts/ops-check.js`, and `node --check scripts/portal-check.js` - passed.
- `npm run check:portal` - passed, including the new acknowledgement assertions.
- `npm run check:ops` - passed, including the expanded renewal confirmation acknowledgement coverage.
- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed after pinning the time-brittle staffing analytics fixture.
- `npm run check:ci` - passed, covering syntax check, production evals, handoff assertions, adapter contract, and all May Goals checks.
- `npm run build` - passed with the existing Vite large-chunk warning. Generated `dist/index.html` asset-hash churn from the build was restored so no generated artifact noise remained.
- Browser smoke - passed on a fresh mock data dir with all provider credentials, live flags, smoke flags, account-manager sends, provider posture, and recovery explicitly disabled. Playwright opened `/share/build/:token`, clicked `mark received`, observed the success message, confirmed the button changed to `received`, and saved `/tmp/callan-renewal-ack-smoke.png`.
- Browser-smoke data proof - passed: `/api/share/build/:token` reported `acknowledgementCount:1`, `confirmationCount:1`, and `latestAcknowledged:true`; the throwaway DB had exactly one acknowledgement action, zero outbound contact events, `customerMessageSent:false` on the acknowledgement, and zero local memory documents.
- Cleanup - the local dev server on ports 8787/5173 was stopped and both ports had no listeners afterward.
- `git diff --check` - passed.

Operator note:
- During an earlier browser-smoke startup, the dev server inherited a real `SUPERMEMORY_API_KEY` and wrote one Supermemory memory document for the synthetic `browser_ack_lead` before the server was stopped. The final browser smoke was rerun on a fresh data dir with provider keys and smoke toggles blanked, and its local DB showed `memoryDocuments:0`.

- Added `acceptRenewalCustomerConfirmation()` for the next customer-facing handoff after acknowledgement. It accepts only visible renewal confirmation receipts owned by the portal lead and refuses acceptance until a matching acknowledgement receipt exists.
- Added durable `renewal_customer_confirmation_accepted` portal-action receipts with inbound contact-event and SSE evidence. Acceptance rows record the source confirmation, acknowledgement, subscription, billing receipt, message receipt, and source portal action while proving `customerMessageSent:false`, `subscriptionChanged:false`, `stripeStateChanged:false`, `paymentLinkCreated:false`, `checkoutLinkCreated:false`, `portalBroadcastSent:false`, and `confirmationAcceptanceLiveSideEffects:false`.
- Made acceptance idempotent per confirmation ID so repeated customer clicks reuse the existing receipt instead of creating duplicate acceptances.
- Added token route `POST /api/share/build/:token/renewal/confirmations/:confirmationId/accept`, with fail-closed 400/404 behavior for wrong action types, missing confirmations, mismatched portal leads, non-visible confirmations, or confirmations that have not been acknowledged yet.
- Added `summarizeRenewalCustomerConfirmationAcceptanceQueue()` and surfaced acceptance receipts through `opsObservability()`, `/api/ops/command-center`, `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalCustomerConfirmationAcceptances`, and an Operations command-center `accept` metric.
- Extended portal subscription-management state so top-level and per-subscription rows expose acceptance counts, latest acceptance, accepted confirmation rows, and no-side-effect safety flags.
- Updated the Share portal confirmation UI so customers first click `mark received`, then get a `looks good` handoff action, which becomes disabled as `accepted` after the receipt is recorded.
- Added focused styling for the confirmation action group so the two-step handoff fits inside the existing subscription card.
- Extended `safe_to_renew.customer_visible_confirmation_requires_applied_or_sent_sources` coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js` to prove acceptance requires acknowledgement, acceptance is idempotent, ops/export/queue/portal surfaces include it, and no outbound customer messages or subscription/payment/Stripe mutations occur.
- Extended `scripts/portal-check.js` so token-scoped portal state proves confirmations can be acknowledged and then accepted by customers.
- Documented the acceptance route and no-live-side-effect boundary in README and updated CLAUDE command guidance.

## Implementation Cycle 167: Operator Follow-Up Queue for Accepted Renewal Confirmations

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/customerPortal.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `src/views/OperationsView.jsx`
- `scripts/ops-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added `createRenewalCustomerConfirmationFollowupWorkItem()` so an accepted renewal confirmation can become an operator-visible local work item instead of disappearing into a customer portal receipt. The function requires a real `renewal_customer_confirmation_accepted` action, validates the source confirmation and acknowledgement chain, dedupes by acceptance ID, and records an `open` `renewal_customer_confirmation_followup_work_item` with recommended next steps, proof requirements, due date, priority, and no-live-side-effect flags.
- Added `resolveRenewalCustomerConfirmationFollowupWorkItem()` so operators can complete, escalate, or cancel that follow-up. Resolution updates the work item, records a `renewal_customer_confirmation_followup_receipt`, appends internal contact/SSE evidence, and keeps customer messages, provider calls, Stripe mutations, payment/checkout links, subscription changes, and portal broadcasts blocked.
- Added admin routes `POST /api/ops/renewal-confirmation-acceptances/:id/followup` and `POST /api/ops/renewal-confirmation-followups/:id/resolve` with fail-closed 400/404 behavior for missing IDs, wrong portal-action types, or invalid outcomes.
- Added `summarizeRenewalCustomerConfirmationFollowupQueue()` and surfaced follow-up work through `opsObservability()`, `/api/ops/command-center`, `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalCustomerConfirmationFollowupWorkItems`, `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalCustomerConfirmationFollowupReceipts`, and an Operations command-center `followup` metric.
- Extended `safe_to_renew.customer_visible_confirmation_requires_applied_or_sent_sources` coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js` to prove follow-up creation requires an acceptance action, creation is idempotent, resolution creates a receipt, queue/observability/export surfaces include the work, and no outbound customer message or subscription/payment/Stripe mutation occurs.
- Documented the accepted-confirmation follow-up routes and no-live-side-effect boundary in README and updated CLAUDE command guidance.

Playwright opened `/`, waited for the Operations command center to finish loading, confirmed the metric strip rendered `FOLLOWUP 1/0`, and saved `/tmp/callan-renewal-followup-ops-smoke.png`.

## Implementation Cycle 168: Customer-Visible Renewal Closeout Packets

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/customerPortal.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `src/views/OperationsView.jsx`
- `src/views/ShareView.jsx`
- `src/styles.css`
- `scripts/ops-check.js`
- `scripts/portal-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Attempted the requested Claude sidecar review again with `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p ...`, but Claude still returned `You've hit your session limit · resets 2:30pm (America/Los_Angeles)`. Work continued locally.
- Added `createRenewalCustomerConfirmationCloseoutPacket()` so a completed operator follow-up receipt can produce a customer-visible renewal closeout packet instead of ending as internal-only proof.
- The closeout packet requires a completed `renewal_customer_confirmation_followup_receipt`, completed follow-up work item, customer acceptance, customer acknowledgement, and the original visible confirmation receipt. Wrong action types, missing source chain rows, escalated/canceled/non-completed receipts, and non-completed work items fail closed.
- Added durable `renewal_customer_confirmation_closeout_packet` portal actions with packet items, customer next steps, source receipt IDs, next review date, contact/trust/SSE evidence, and explicit no-live-side-effect flags for customer messages, portal broadcasts, providers, Stripe, subscriptions, payment links, and checkout links.
- Made closeout packet creation idempotent by follow-up receipt so repeated operator clicks reuse the existing customer-visible packet.
- Added admin route `POST /api/ops/renewal-confirmation-followup-receipts/:id/closeout`.
- Added `summarizeRenewalCustomerConfirmationCloseoutPacketQueue()` and surfaced packets through `opsObservability()`, `/api/ops/command-center`, `exportOperationsData().tables.renewalCustomerConfirmationCloseoutPackets`, and an Operations command-center `closeout` metric.
- Extended Share portal subscription state so top-level and per-subscription rows expose closeout packet counts, latest closeout packet metadata, and each confirmation row can render `renewal closed` plus the closeout summary.
- Extended `safe_to_renew.customer_visible_confirmation_requires_applied_or_sent_sources` coverage in `scripts/ops-check.js` to prove closeout packets require follow-up receipts, dedupe correctly, surface in queue/observability/export/portal state, and do not message customers or mutate subscriptions, payments, or Stripe.
- Extended `scripts/portal-check.js` so token-scoped portal state proves the full renewal confirmation path can progress through acknowledgement, acceptance, operator follow-up, resolution, and customer-visible closeout.
- Fixed a real flake in `scripts/ops-check.js` job polling: if a job reached a wanted status exactly at the timeout boundary, the final row check now returns it instead of throwing a false timeout while reporting `status=completed`.
- Documented the closeout packet route and no-live-side-effect boundary in README and updated CLAUDE command guidance.

- Extended `portfolioOperatingModel.planCustomerRetentionPlaybook()` so retention playbooks can be planned from either a remediation closeout receipt or a customer-visible `renewal_customer_confirmation_closeout_packet`.
- Added renewal closeout validation: the portal action must exist, belong to the expected type, be `visible_to_customer`, and resolve its source lead/subscription evidence before it can seed a playbook.
- Added explicit service-business, customer, job, offer-bundle, finance-rollup, workspace, and lead boundary checks so a renewal packet cannot silently attach to the wrong operating record.
- Renewal closeout playbooks now default to `renewal_closeout_retention`, `renewal_health_watch`, churn risk `0.28`, and subscription annualized expected retained revenue when no explicit values are supplied.
- Fixed the churn-risk defaulting bug where `Number(null)` turned an omitted churn risk into `0` and bypassed the renewal default.
- Added renewal closeout packet IDs, source lead IDs, subscription IDs, next-review dates, and no-live-side-effect safety flags to the playbook payload, customer metadata, workspace metadata, and portfolio event metadata.
- Added May Goals regression coverage proving a renewal closeout packet creates a local-only retention playbook, annualizes a $99/month subscription to $118,800 cents retained revenue, records the right safety flags, updates metadata, and can dry-run execution without customer messages, offers, payment links, billing changes, or booking side effects.
- Updated snapshot/export assertions so the operating model now expects two retention playbooks and four retention playbook receipts, including the new renewal closeout retention path.
- Documented the renewal closeout-to-retention-playbook bridge in README and added `npm run check:maygoals` guidance to CLAUDE.md.

## Blockers

- Full external realization of the north star requires real provider credentials, phone/email/payment/browser/builder accounts, legal/product decisions for non-website services, vendor/subcontractor relationships, and real customers. These are not blockers to making repo progress now because the next slice can be implemented and verified locally.
- Current goal is intentionally impossible; completion cannot be claimed until every requirement above is either implemented and verified or explicitly impossible with hard evidence.

- Added `buildRetentionCommandWorkItemProofPacket()` and `portfolioOperatingModel.collectRetentionCommandWorkItemProofPacket()` to collect approved retention command work-item lifecycle receipts into an evidence-only packet.
- The packet records source command receipt IDs, work-item IDs, lifecycle receipt IDs, required proof keys, operator-reviewed proof keys, local proof blockers, counts, and `retention_command_work_item_proof_packet_safety`.
- Kept proof semantics intentionally conservative: operator-reviewed provider-smoke and adapter work items are exposed as local review evidence, while `liveGateSemantics.canClearCustomerRetentionLivePreflight`, `liveProviderSmokeSatisfied`, and `liveAdapterImplementationSatisfied` remain false so local review cannot masquerade as a real provider smoke or implemented live adapter.
- Added API/client/UI wiring with `POST /api/portfolio/retention-command-center/:id/proof-packet`, `api.collectRetentionCommandWorkItemProofPacket()`, Portfolio proof packet collection buttons, proof-key tags, and a live-gate-not-cleared signal.

- Extended MayGoals coverage to collect the seeded provider-smoke, live-adapter, and run-mode proof packet, assert no live-gate bypass, assert no customer/provider/adapter/job/finance side effects, and assert snapshot/export/UI/API/serialization coverage.
- Updated README to document the evidence-only proof-packet path.

- Release receipts require an active local lease, preserve the proof key and receipt ancestry, mark the lease released, and keep blocker resolution false until an explicit approval receipt happens later.
- Added API/client/UI wiring with `POST /api/portfolio/retention-command-work-items/:id/claim`, `POST /api/portfolio/retention-command-work-items/:id/release`, `api.claimRetentionCommandWorkItem()`, `api.releaseRetentionCommandWorkItem()`, and Portfolio claim/release controls beside approve.
- Extended the retention command-center snapshot and redacted ops export with claimed/released lifecycle counts while keeping approved proof-packet semantics unchanged.
- Extended MayGoals coverage to claim and release a seeded retention command work item, then separately approve all three proof work items, asserting lifecycle count expansion from 3 to 5, no blocker resolution during lease changes, no side effects, UI/API coverage, snapshot/export coverage, and serialization evidence.
- Updated README to document retention command local leases.

- Added `portfolio_retention_command_work_item_lease_maintenance_receipts` as a durable read-only maintenance ledger over retention command work-item lease state.
- Added `recordRetentionCommandWorkItemLeaseMaintenance()` to snapshot work-item counts, active/stale/released/expired lease counts, next lease expiry, recommended actions, evidence, summary text, workspace metadata, and a portfolio event without mutating leases or crossing live side-effect boundaries.
- Added `retention_command_work_item_lease_maintenance_safety` proving the maintenance receipt is telemetry only: no customer messages, offers, prices, payment links, bookings, finance mutation, provider calls, adapter invokes, jobs, command-receipt mutation, cohort mutation, or local lease updates.
- Wired API/client/UI support through `POST /api/portfolio/retention-command-work-items/lease-maintenance`, `api.recordRetentionCommandLeaseMaintenance()`, a Portfolio command-center action, and a maintenance receipt count in the Retention Cohort Command Center grid.
- Extended snapshot counts, table hydration, coverage/reset lists, retention command-center fields, source receipt ancestry, redacted ops export tables, action commands, and redaction-manifest proof counts for the new maintenance receipt table.
- Extended MayGoals coverage to record a maintenance receipt after the fixture sweep, prove `no_active_leases`, assert released/expired lease counts, verify recommended `no_active_lease_action_required`, cover UI/API source tokens, and require snapshot/export/manifest/serialized proof.
- Updated README to document read-only retention command lease maintenance receipts alongside claim/release/expiry/sweep proof.

## Implementation Cycle 229: Retention Command Lease Maintenance Scheduler

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/env.js`
- `server/ops.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `README.md`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Continued locally because the requested Claude sidecar was still capped before its 8:30 AM PDT reset window.
- Added an opt-in durable recurring ops job type, `ops.retention_command_lease_maintenance`, for recording retention command lease maintenance receipts from the scheduler/worker path.
- Added `RETENTION_COMMAND_LEASE_MAINTENANCE_ENABLED` and `RETENTION_COMMAND_LEASE_MAINTENANCE_INTERVAL_MS` env controls. The job is disabled by default so it does not create a new safe-to-sell production blocker until an operator explicitly enables it.
- Implemented `runRetentionCommandLeaseMaintenanceJob()`, `enqueueRetentionCommandLeaseMaintenance()`, `startRetentionCommandLeaseMaintenanceScheduler()`, and `stopRetentionCommandLeaseMaintenanceScheduler()`.
- Wired the new job into durable job handlers, server boot scheduler startup, recurring ops scheduler health, and safe-to-sell stale-maintenance refresh.
- The job snapshots retention command-center rows with work items, records read-only lease maintenance receipts through `recordRetentionCommandWorkItemLeaseMaintenance()`, and persists job provenance in receipt evidence with `retention_command_work_item_lease_maintenance_job_safety` / `ops.retention_command_lease_maintenance`.
- Extended MayGoals coverage so the fixture records a direct maintenance receipt and a scheduler/job maintenance receipt, proves the opt-in scheduler health row is present but disabled by default, and updates snapshot/export/manifest counts from one maintenance receipt to two.
- Updated README to document the opt-in recurring maintenance job and its default-disabled production posture.

Verification evidence:

- `node --check server/index.js` - passed.
- `node --check server/env.js` - passed.
- `node --check scripts/maygoals-check.js` - passed.

- `npm run check:ci` - passed, covering syntax check, production evals, handoff assertions, adapter contract, and all MayGoals checks.
- `npm run build` - passed with the existing Vite large-chunk warning. Generated `dist/index.html` asset-hash churn from the build was restored.

The local loop can queue blockers, claim/release/expire leases, record sweep and maintenance receipts, monitor scheduler health without new default production blockers, and export redacted proof. - Preserved the side-effect boundary: the control records/queues local lease-maintenance work only and does not call providers, adapters, customer channels, finance, bookings, or live fulfillment.
- Extended MayGoals source coverage to assert the admin route, API client, Operations button/action state, and queued-result copy exist alongside the already verified job handler and maintenance receipt proof.
- Updated README provider-smoke/scheduler docs to document the route and Operations control.

## Implementation Cycle 231: Retention Command Lease Maintenance HTTP Boundary Proof

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Turned the delegated Claude review's best finding into executable proof: the new ops route now has HTTP-boundary coverage inside `check:maygoals`, not just source assertions.
- Added `ops.retention_command_lease_maintenance_route_boundary` to boot a throwaway mock server on an ephemeral local port with a strong `ADMIN_API_TOKEN`.
- The route proof disables background schedulers, uses a temporary `DATA_DIR`, waits for `/api/ping`, and tears the child server plus temp data down after the check.
- Verified unauthenticated admin access fails with HTTP 401 and `ADMIN_AUTH_REQUIRED` while confirming the admin token is configured.
- Verified the authenticated enqueue branch returns HTTP 202, `accepted: true`, `scope: retention_command_lease_maintenance`, a queued job id, and `inserted: true`.
- Verified immediate duplicate enqueue dedupes through the route with `inserted: false` and the same job id.
- Verified the authenticated `runNow: true` branch returns HTTP 200 with a local no-candidate maintenance report, zero receipts, no external side effects, no maintenance receipt recorded, and no job enqueue.
- Verified scoped no-match filters remain safe with zero receipts and `localMaintenanceReceiptRecorded: false`.
- Fixed the route smoke to use `localhost` instead of `127.0.0.1` because this Express boot listens on IPv6-any on macOS, and widened the cold-start timeout to cover SQLite setup without masking HTTP failures.

Verification evidence:
- `node --check scripts/maygoals-check.js` - passed.
- `npm run check:maygoals` - first failed while proving the smoke was too strict for macOS IPv6/cold-start behavior; after switching to `localhost` and a 30s wait, passed with `ops.retention_command_lease_maintenance_route_boundary`.
- `npm run check:ci` - passed, including the new HTTP-boundary MayGoals check.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `git status --short dist node_modules/.vite/deps/_metadata.json .playwright-mcp` - clean/no output.
- `ps -axo pid,ppid,stat,etime,command | rg 'callan-retention-route-check|node server/index.js|maygoals-check'` - no lingering route-check or MayGoals server process.

Update after Cycle 231: The retention command lease-maintenance control now has route-level proof for admin rejection, authenticated enqueue, route-level idempotent dedupe, run-now safety, scoped no-match filters, and child-server cleanup. The next local slice can use the same route-boundary pattern for scheduler enable/stop lifecycle proof, active/stale lease status proof before the sweep, or move back toward provider-smoke/live-adapter evidence closeout.

## Implementation Cycle 250: Provider Smoke Side-Effect Claim Rejection

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/db.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Updated readiness provider-smoke receipt creation so operator-submitted side-effect claims are preserved in the provider health event detail instead of being silently normalized away.
- Added explicit `sideEffectClaims`, `unsafeSideEffectClaimed`, and `liveBlockedByOperatorSideEffectClaim` semantics for provider-smoke receipts.
- Made provider-smoke receipts fail closed when the proof claims any provider call, adapter invocation, durable job enqueue, side-effect flag change, customer message, budget movement, or external side effect, even if run mode, live flags, smoke pass, and operator verification are otherwise present.
- Extended provider-smoke proof packets to expose and reject those unsafe side-effect claims before a receipt can match as verified live provider smoke.
- Added in-process MayGoals coverage proving side-effect-claimed provider smoke stays `mock_ok`, does not match verified live evidence, and cannot auto-refresh readiness.
- Added HTTP route-boundary coverage proving the provider-smoke receipt route returns a non-live receipt when the POST body claims provider calls, jobs, customer messages, and external side effects, while actual Callan-side safety fields remain false.

Verification evidence:
- `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --print ...` completed a bounded read-only scout; it recommended the next larger route-boundary slice for the existing merge-queue secret-provider smoke chain.
- `node --check server/db.js` - passed.
- `node --check scripts/maygoals-check.js` - passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed, including unsafe provider-smoke side-effect receipt assertions and `ops.readiness_command_evidence_route_boundary` unsafe-route assertions.
- `npm run check:portfolio-readiness-browser` - passed; verified the normal UI-created provider-smoke receipt still becomes verified, keeps `Provider Live Smoke Receipt`, clears the live gate, and preserves the adapter evidence path.
- `npm run check:ci` - passed, covering syntax, evals, handoff checks, adapter contract, and MayGoals.
- `npm run check:ops` - passed with expected mocked provider/fixture warnings and final `ok: true`.
- `git diff -- dist/index.html` - empty.

Update after Cycle 250: The readiness provider-smoke route now records unsafe operator side-effect claims as first-class fail-closed evidence. A malicious or mistaken proof packet can no longer claim a provider call, job, customer message, or external side effect and still be treated like verified live smoke. The next high-value slice, from the bounded Claude scout, is to add HTTP boundary coverage for the existing merge-queue runtime secret-provider smoke execution gate so that secret-provider smoke and credential-release semantics are proven over the actual route layer, not only in process.

## Implementation Cycle 288: Live Execute Runtime Guardrail Recheck Route Boundary

Implemented:

  - live side-effect flags for public outreach, payments, and builds,
  - and `allowLiveBoardDecisionExecution`.

- Preserved the existing terminal adapter boundary: when all execute-time runtime guardrails pass, live execution still fails at `service_decision_execution_controller live adapter is not implemented`.

- Seeded the proof from Cycle 287's real `ready_for_live` preflight receipt, then executed `mode:"live"` in an isolated child with `RUN_MODE=mock`, live side-effect flags false, and `allowLiveBoardDecisionExecution:false`.
- Proved the live execute route rejects unauthenticated callers with `401`, accepts an operator token but returns `500`, and reports `run_mode`, `side_effect_flag`, and `live_board_execution_guardrail` blockers before reaching the unimplemented adapter throw.
- Updated the Cycle 286 assertion to recognize the new structured `ready_live_preflight_receipt` blocker text when no ready preflight exists.
- Asserted local deltas: zero service-decision execution receipt writes from the blocked live execute, zero release preflight receipt changes, zero review receipt changes, zero live-readiness evidence artifacts, zero live-adapter receipts, zero compensation plans, zero compensation receipts, zero readiness reconciliations, zero decision distributions/work items/work-item receipts, unchanged contact/call/payment/subscription/job/provider-credential/acquisition-action/capital-allocation/provider-fallback side-effect counters, and no common token/webhook secret patterns in route responses.

## Implementation Cycle 289: Durable Live Execute Denial Receipts

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/db.js`
- `server/index.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added `portfolio_service_decision_live_execute_denials` as an append-only audit table for blocked service-decision `mode:"live"` attempts.
- Added indexes, snapshot counts, snapshot hydration, tenant scoped-table coverage, and workspace relationship leak checks for the new denial receipt family.
- Added `portfolioOperatingModel.recordServiceDecisionLiveExecuteDenial()` to persist a safe denial packet before the live execute branch throws.
- Preserved fail-closed route behavior: runtime guardrail failures still return `500`, and the unimplemented `service_decision_execution_controller` live adapter boundary still returns `500`.
- Persisted only safe denial metadata: source dry-run execution receipt, ready preflight receipt, actor, run mode, denial reason, gate keys/results, proof key names, external-side-effect false safety, and evidence references. Raw proof payloads and provider secrets are not persisted in the denial request body.
- Added durable denials for both live execute failure classes:
  - `runtime_guardrails` when execute-time run mode, side-effect flags, ready preflight receipt, or board guardrail fail,
  - `adapter_not_implemented` when runtime guardrails pass but the controller live adapter is still intentionally absent.
- Added read surfaces:
  - `GET /api/portfolio/service-decision-live-execute-denials`,
  - `GET /api/portfolio/service-decision-executions/:id/live-execute-denials`,
  - and embedded `liveExecuteDenials` in service-decision fusion and execution detail responses.
- Added `filterServiceDecisionLiveExecuteDenials()` with workspace, service business, fusion receipt, source/ready execution receipt, provider, status, denial reason, and run-mode filters.
- Added `ops.portfolio_service_decision_live_execute_blocked_attempt_writes_durable_denial_receipt_route_boundary` to prove denied live execution is now auditable without becoming a live side effect.
- Updated the Cycle 288 re-gate proof to assert one durable denial row is written while service-decision execution receipts and all side-effect tables remain unchanged.
- The new MayGoals route proof exercises both denial classes:
  - mock runtime with disabled live flags and no board guardrail writes a `runtime_guardrails` denial with `run_mode`, `side_effect_flag`, and `live_board_execution_guardrail` blockers;
  - production-live test runtime with live flags and board guardrail enabled writes an `adapter_not_implemented` denial and still fails before any adapter invocation.
- Proved denial read routes reject unauthenticated access with `401`, return `200` for an operator token, include the newly written runtime and adapter denials, filter by `denialReason`, embed in execution detail, and return `404` for a missing execution seed.
- Asserted local deltas: exactly two denial rows for the new proof, zero service-decision execution receipt writes from the blocked live executes, zero release preflight/review/evidence/adapter/compensation/reconciliation/distribution/work-item writes, unchanged contact/call/payment/subscription/job/provider-credential/acquisition-action/capital-allocation/provider-fallback side-effect counters, and no common token/webhook secret patterns in route responses or persisted denial rows.

- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed after the harness fix, including `ops.portfolio_service_decision_live_execute_blocked_attempt_writes_durable_denial_receipt_route_boundary`.

The controller still cannot perform live work, but every denied live attempt can be read back by source execution/fusion/run mode/denial reason without creating execution receipts or any external side effects. ## Implementation Cycle 290: Live Execute Denial Incident Rollup

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/index.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added a read-only operator-facing incident rollup for repeated blocked service-decision live execute attempts.
- Added `aggregateServiceDecisionLiveExecuteDenialIncidents()` to group already-filtered denial receipts by workspace, service business, and service-decision fusion receipt.
- Each incident summarizes:
  - total denied attempt count,
  - counts by denial reason,
  - distinct blocker keys,
  - distinct run modes,
  - distinct source dry-run execution receipt ids,
  - distinct ready-live-preflight receipt ids,
  - first/last denied timestamps plus ISO strings,
  - latest denial id/reason/run mode,
  - repeated attempt count,
  - read-only/action-required flags,
  - and `externalSideEffects:false` unless a persisted denial ever claims otherwise.
- Added `GET /api/portfolio/service-decision-live-execute-denial-incidents`.
- The new route reuses `filterServiceDecisionLiveExecuteDenials()` first, so raw denial lists and incident rollups share the same workspace, service business, fusion receipt, source/ready execution receipt, provider, status, denial reason, and run-mode filters.
- Added `ops.portfolio_service_decision_live_execute_denial_incident_rollup_route_boundary`.
- The route proof uses the denial rows written by the preceding live-execute denial checks, selects a stable incident seed by grouped receipt ids and expected reason counts, and avoids timestamp-window selection bugs.
- Proved unauthenticated incident reads return `401`, operator reads return `200`, the rollup kind is `service_decision_live_execute_denial_incident_rollup`, grouped attempt counts match the underlying receipts, runtime and adapter denial reason counts match, repeated attempt count is correct, first/last timestamps match the receipt group, and distinct blocker keys include `run_mode`, `side_effect_flag`, `live_board_execution_guardrail`, and `service_decision_execution_controller_live_adapter_not_implemented`.
- Proved `denialReason=adapter_not_implemented` filters the incident down to adapter-only attempts and a missing service-business filter returns an empty read-only rollup.
- Asserted local deltas: zero service-decision execution receipt writes, zero denial receipt writes, zero release preflight/review/evidence/adapter/compensation/reconciliation/distribution/work-item writes, unchanged contact/call/payment/subscription/job/provider-credential/acquisition-action/capital-allocation/provider-fallback side-effect counters, and no common token/webhook secret patterns in rollup responses.

- `npm run check:ci` - passed, covering syntax, evals, handoff checks, adapter contract, and MayGoals.

The app can answer "how many times did this business try to go live, why did it fail, what blockers repeat, and when did it last happen" without scanning individual denial rows and without creating any new side effects. A strong next slice is to link these incident summaries into readiness command center detail/list payloads, still read-only, so the existing command center becomes the unified operator cockpit for readiness blockers and denied live-execute attempts.

## Implementation Cycle 294: Live Execute Denial Urgency Bands

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/db.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added `denialUrgency` to the readiness command center `liveReleaseBlockerDigest`.
- The urgency packet is read-only and computes:
  - urgency band,
  - urgency score,
  - denial count,
  - repeated attempt count,
  - distinct blocker count,
  - latest denial timestamp,
  - latest denial age,
  - 24-hour stale threshold metadata,
  - stale boolean,
  - and side-effect safety flags.
- Added repeated-denial bands:
  - `high_repeated_denials` for repeated blocked-live attempts,
  - `critical_repeated_denials` for heavily repeated attempts,
  - plus `stale_unresolved_denial`, `recent_single_denial`, and `none`.
- Propagated the urgency band onto the digest's `export_live_execute_denial_provenance` next action so operator queues can prioritize the handoff packet action.
- Extended `ops.portfolio_readiness_command_center_live_release_blocker_digest_route_boundary` to prove the urgency shape, repeated-denial band, positive score, read-only safety, and export-action urgency metadata.

## Implementation Cycle 295: Live Release Blocker Queue

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/index.js`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added read-only `GET /api/portfolio/readiness-command-center/live-release-blockers`.
- The queue filters readiness commands to only service decisions whose `liveReleaseBlockerDigest.releasePreflightBlocked` is true.
- The queue sorts blocked commands by:
  - denial urgency score descending,
  - live-execute denial count descending,
  - blocker count descending,
  - then command id ascending for deterministic ties.
- The response returns a service-decision envelope with `kind: service_decision_live_release_blocker_queue`, queue count, top urgency score, top urgency band, and the full command digest/action metadata operators need for handoff.
- Added `ops.portfolio_readiness_command_center_live_release_blocker_queue_route_boundary`.
- The proof exercises unauthenticated rejection, operator read access, seed command presence, urgency ordering, blocked-only queue membership, export-endpoint/action continuity, read-only row and side-effect deltas, and secret redaction.

## Implementation Cycle 306: Queue Export Review Readback Lane

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/index.js`
- `src/views/PortfolioView.jsx`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `scripts/portfolio-readiness-browser-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Normalized live-release blocker queue export review receipt API payloads with camelCase aliases for `filterSummary`, `integrityManifest`, and `commandIds` while preserving the existing row fields.
- Extended `GET /api/portfolio/readiness-command-center/live-release-blockers/export-reviews` so receipt readbacks can be filtered by the same queue lane params used by exports: urgency band, denial reason, and stale-only.
- Added a Decision Command Center checksum acknowledgement readback lane that lists recent review receipts for the selected queue filter.
- The Portfolio export handler now checks for an existing receipt for the exact exported checksum and marks the export as already acknowledged when applicable.
- The `Acknowledge checksum` action now refuses to run a duplicate review for an already acknowledged export and disables to `Checksum acknowledged` after the receipt appears, keeping release gates and blockers untouched.
- Extended the MayGoals route proof to post the same review twice and prove the receipt id is stable, the row delta remains one, and the filtered readback route returns only the selected stale/runtime lane.
- Extended the browser proof to assert the visible stale-lane receipt readback and disabled duplicate acknowledgement state.

Verification evidence:
- `node --check server/index.js` - passed.
- `node --check scripts/maygoals-check.js` - passed.
- `node --check scripts/portfolio-readiness-browser-check.js` - passed.
- `npm run check:maygoals` - passed, including duplicate receipt and filtered readback assertions.
- `npm run build` - passed with the existing large chunk warning; Vite rewrote `dist/index.html` hashed asset references and they were restored so no build artifact churn remains.
- `npm run check:portfolio-readiness-browser` - passed with Playwright fallback, seeded readiness data, no console issues, visible receipt readback, and disabled duplicate checksum acknowledgement.
- `npm run check:ci` - passed, covering syntax, evals, handoff checks, adapter contract, and MayGoals.

Update after Cycle 306: Operators can now see recent checksum acknowledgements for the active live-release blocker queue lane and cannot accidentally loop the same review after a receipt exists. A strong next slice is to add a queue-receipt comparison surface that highlights when the current redacted export checksum differs from the latest acknowledged checksum for the same lane, so operators can tell whether new blockers appeared after review without clearing any release gate.

## Implementation Cycle 307: Queue Export Acknowledgement Drift Comparison

Status: implemented and verified locally.

Changed files:
- `server/index.js`
- `src/views/PortfolioView.jsx`
- `scripts/maygoals-check.js`
- `scripts/portfolio-readiness-browser-check.js`
- `.codex-maygoals-progress.md`

Implemented:
- Added server-side comparison metadata for redacted live-release blocker queue exports so each export can report whether the current checksum is unacknowledged, matches the latest acknowledgement, or changed since the latest acknowledgement for the same queue lane.
- Added a focused receipt lookup helper for live-release blocker queue export reviews using the same lane filters as the export route: urgency band, denial reason, and stale-only.
- Extended the Portfolio Decision Command Center export state with acknowledgement drift text that shows the current checksum, latest acknowledged checksum when different, and a plain-language status without implying that any release blocker was cleared.
- Updated the acknowledge action so a newly written review receipt immediately marks the current export as matching the latest acknowledgement while preserving all live-release gates.
- Extended the MayGoals route proof to seed an older different checksum, prove the next export reports `changed_since_latest_acknowledgement`, acknowledge the actual current export, re-export, and prove the comparison reports `matches_latest_acknowledgement`.
- Extended the browser readiness proof to exercise the stale blocker queue export, acknowledge the checksum, and verify the rendered acknowledgement comparison state in the UI.

Verification evidence:
- `node --check server/index.js` - passed.
- `node --check scripts/maygoals-check.js` - passed.
- `node --check scripts/portfolio-readiness-browser-check.js` - passed.
